target.title: fix(thinking): let authoritative provider profiles override stale catalog reasoning=false
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84880. Subagent spawns and `/think` validation reject non-`off` thinking on canonical `openai/*` and `openai-codex/*` GPT-5 reasoning models when a stale catalog entry has `reasoning: false`. `resolveThinkingProfile` short-circuits to off-only on `reasoning === false` before consulting the provider plugin profile, so persisted catalogs that lag behind runtime augmentation force `Use one of: off.` even for models the provider knows support `high`/`xhigh`.

Introduces an `overridesCatalogReasoning?: boolean` flag on `ProviderThinkingProfile`. When a provider returns a profile with this flag set, the resolver treats the plugin as the canonical source and uses its level list ahead of the catalog opt-out. Providers opt in **per model**, so non-reasoning models (e.g. `openai/chat-latest`, `openai/gpt-5.3-chat-latest`) still honor catalog `reasoning: false`.

The OpenAI extension opts in for GPT-5 reasoning model refs and explicitly excludes non-reasoning chat-latest variants. The classifier is **suffix-aware**: any `*-chat-latest` model is catalog-authoritative unless it appears in the explicit `OPENAI_REASONING_CHAT_LATEST_MODEL_IDS = ["gpt-5.1-chat-latest", "gpt-5.2-chat-latest"]` opt-in list (matches bundled catalog reasoning flags in `extensions/openai/openclaw.plugin.json`).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `sessions_spawn(model="openai-codex/gpt-5.5", thinking="high"|"xhigh")` and `sessions_spawn(model="openai/gpt-5.4", thinking="high")` are rejected with `Thinking level "high" is not supported for openai/gpt-5.5. Use one of: off.` even though the canonical OpenAI/Codex catalog lists these models as reasoning-capable — see live v2026.5.20 repro logs in [issue #84880](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84880). The patch reorders `resolveThinkingProfile` so an authoritative provider profile (now flagged via `overridesCatalogReasoning`) wins over stale persisted catalog `reasoning: false` rows.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 25.5.0 (Darwin arm64), Node 24.13.0, pnpm 11.1.0; repository checked out at `fix/subagent-thinking-overrides-catalog-reasoning` head `69986fc5` rebased on `origin/main` `f483f59b`; runtime probe driving the patched modules end-to-end through the same `resolveOpenAIThinkingProfile` / `resolveOpenAICodexThinkingProfile` entry points that the live Gateway calls on every `sessions_spawn` validation in `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts:794` and `src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts:1172`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
git checkout fix/subagent-thinking-overrides-catalog-reasoning
node --import tsx probe-thinking.mjs
```

The probe is a small driver script that imports the patched `extensions/openai/thinking-policy.ts` and calls the classifier for each acceptance scenario from #84880 plus the chat-latest regression guards from the ClawSweeper review, then checks the resolved `levels[]` and `overridesCatalogReasoning` flag against expected values.

**Evidence after fix**: Live terminal output from `node --import tsx probe-thinking.mjs` after applying the patch — copied verbatim, no redaction needed since the probe data is synthetic:

```
=== Runtime probe: extensions/openai/thinking-policy.ts ===

PASS | [#84880 §1] openai/gpt-5.4 high
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | [#84880 §2] openai-codex/gpt-5.5 high
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | [#84880 §3] openai-codex/gpt-5.5 xhigh
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/gpt-5.5 minimal/low/medium/high/xhigh
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | [ClawSweeper P1] openai/chat-latest
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=false (want false)
PASS | [ClawSweeper P1] openai/gpt-5-chat-latest
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=false (want false)
PASS | [ClawSweeper P1] openai/gpt-5.3-chat-latest
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=false (want false)
PASS | openai/gpt-5.1-chat-latest (reasoning catalog row)
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/gpt-5.2-chat-latest (reasoning catalog row)
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/o3-mini
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/gpt-5-pro
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/gpt-4.1
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=false (want false)

Summary: all 12 probes PASS.
```

**Observed result after fix**: 12/12 probes pass. For the canonical GPT-5 reasoning model refs the patch flips `overridesCatalogReasoning` to `true` and surfaces the full `[off, minimal, low, medium, high]` level set (plus `xhigh` on the appropriate models), so a stale catalog row marked `reasoning: false` no longer triggers the off-only short-circuit. For non-reasoning chat-latest variants (`chat-latest`, `gpt-5-chat-latest`, `gpt-5.3-chat-latest`) and legacy non-reasoning models (`gpt-4.1`) the classifier returns `overridesCatalogReasoning=false`, so a stale catalog `reasoning: false` row still wins and the runtime keeps the off-only path documented by the catalog opt-out.

Cross-check against the bundled `extensions/openai/openclaw.plugin.json` shows the classifier verdict matches the catalog truth for every chat-latest variant in the bundle:

| Model | Catalog reasoning | Classifier verdict | Stale-row override |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gpt-5-chat-latest` | `false` | non-authoritative | catalog wins (off-only) |
| `gpt-5.1-chat-latest` | `true` | authoritative | overrides stale `false` |
| `gpt-5.2-chat-latest` | `true` | authoritative | overrides stale `false` |
| `gpt-5.3-chat-latest` | `false` | non-authoritative | catalog wins (off-only) |
| `chat-latest` | `false` | non-authoritative | catalog wins (off-only) |
| `gpt-5.5`, `gpt-5.4`, `gpt-5-codex`, `o3-mini`, … | `true` | authoritative | overrides stale `false` |
| `gpt-4.1`, `gpt-4o`, … | `false` | non-authoritative | catalog wins (off-only) |

**What was not tested**: A redacted `sessions_spawn` live run against a custom-built Gateway binary in my deployment — the probe exercises the same classifier and resolver entry points the live Gateway calls, but I did not build and install a custom Gateway binary into my OrbStack VM for this PR. Also out of scope: the Ollama Cloud variant raised by @SplyzerRB in #84880 where the subagent ignores explicit `thinking: "off"` — that path forwards `thinking` even when the spawn arg is `off` and is a separate runtime bug that does not share a code path with the OpenAI/Codex catalog-override issue this PR fixes.

## What changes

- **`src/plugins/provider-thinking.types.ts`** — add `overridesCatalogReasoning?: boolean` to `ProviderThinkingProfile`.
- **`src/auto-reply/thinking.ts`** — reorder `resolveThinkingProfile`: when the plugin profile is authoritative, it wins over catalog `reasoning: false`; otherwise the existing off-only short-circuit applies.
- **`extensions/openai/thinking-policy.ts`** — suffix-aware classifier for chat-latest variants; flags reasoning model refs as authoritative via `isOpenAIReasoningModelId`.
- **`docs/tools/thinking.md`** and **`docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md`** — document the new opt-in flag.
- **`docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`** — regenerated via the API baseline generator.

Additionally — **`fix(plugins): break plugin-registry-snapshot ↔ plugin-metadata-snapshot.types import cycle`** (`69986fc5`):
- Pre-existing madge cycle on main introduced by upstream `bf64de91`. Verified by reverting this PR's diff and re-running the architecture check on plain `origin/main` — cycle still reproduces.
- Extract `PluginRegistrySnapshotSource` to a new leaf module `src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.types.ts`; `plugin-registry-snapshot.ts` re-exports the same name so no downstream import changes. Happy to split into a separate PR if maintainers prefer.

## Notes

- Related to PR #84902 (NianJiuZst), which takes the same architectural direction. This PR is an independent attempt with additional regression-guard tests covering the `gpt-5.3-chat-latest` chat-latest variant raised by ClawSweeper review. Happy to defer to or close in favor of #84902 if maintainers prefer that thread.

target.title: fix(config): validate denyCommands after gateway.nodes writes

## Summary
- validate `gateway.nodes.denyCommands` during `openclaw config set` before write
- catch direct leaf writes plus ancestor-object writes such as `gateway.nodes={...}`
- keep custom commands valid when they are introduced via `allowCommands` in the same update

## Problem
`denyCommands` accepted unknown entries silently on `config set`. A typo like `denyCommands=["sendd"]` looked blocked but was ignored at runtime. Parent-object writes could also bypass an exact-leaf check.

## Testing
- `pnpm vitest run src/config/validate-deny-commands.test.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts src/gateway/gateway-misc.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/cli/config-cli.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts src/config/validate-deny-commands.ts src/config/validate-deny-commands.test.ts`

Closes #50011

target.title: fix(gateway): defer provider auth prewarm after startup
target.body:
## Summary

This PR narrows issue #63229 to the startup ordering problem we reproduced locally: provider auth-state prewarm can run right as the gateway reports ready and compete with the first `sessions_spawn` request.

The change moves provider auth-state prewarm into delayed gateway-lifetime work that starts after sidecars are ready. The auth-profile failure hook still clears and rewarms provider auth state, the delayed warm uses current runtime config, and shutdown cancellation prevents stopped prewarm work from publishing stale prepared auth state.

The provider-auth handle is intentionally separate from Gmail post-ready sidecars, so a Gmail watcher hot reload cannot disable the long-lived auth failure hook.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts` - passed, 53 tests across 3 files.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts` - passed, 231 tests across 10 files.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts` - passed, 64 tests across 4 files during final review.
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=agentic-control-plane-runtime OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=<35-path include file> node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts` - passed, 302 tests across 34 files on the earlier head for the same gateway/runtime surface.
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD && git diff --check` - passed.
- `codex review --base upstream/main` - clean after the lifecycle fix; no introduced correctness issues found.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Early gateway `sessions_spawn` should not wait behind expensive provider auth-state prewarm when the gateway has just become ready.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway against local vLLM serving `Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct` as `qwen-a` on loopback.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started vLLM, started the patched gateway with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, auth disabled on loopback, and default model `vllm/qwen-a`; waited for `/health`, then immediately invoked `/tools/invoke` with `sessions_spawn`.

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output and redacted runtime log from the local OpenClaw/vLLM repro:

```text
$ curl -s -o /tmp/spawn-response.json -w 'HTTP %{http_code} time_total=%{time_total}\n' \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  --data '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":"spawn-1","method":"tools/invoke","params":{"tool":"sessions_spawn","arguments":{"prompt":"say ready","model":"vllm/qwen-a"}}}' \
  http://127.0.0.1:<gateway-port>/tools/invoke
HTTP 200 time_total=0.443812

$ jq '.result.status' /tmp/spawn-response.json
"accepted"
```

```text
[redacted gateway log]
gateway ready
provider auth state pre-warmed in 36875ms
```

Observed result after fix: The first session spawn was accepted before provider auth-state prewarm completed, proving the startup/prewarm ordering regression is removed for the reproduced slice.

What was not tested: I did not rerun the original large-model repro, and this PR does not claim to fix separate healthy-vLLM false-timeout or fallback-cascade symptoms from #63229.

target.title: fix(nextcloud-talk): parse rich object file shares from webhook payload
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #29152

When a user shares a file or image in Nextcloud Talk, the bot webhook receives the message but the NC Talk plugin was not parsing the rich object parameters — so the agent never saw the attachment.

NC Talk encodes file shares as rich object messages where `object.content` is a JSON string:
```json
{"message":"{file}","parameters":{"file":{"type":"file","id":"12345","name":"IMG_123.jpg","path":"Talk/IMG_123.jpg","link":"https://cloud.example.com/f/12345","mimetype":"image/jpeg","preview-available":"yes"}}}
```

Previously `payloadToInboundMessage()` passed this raw JSON string as `text`, leaving `mediaUrls` unpopulated.

## Changes

### `monitor.ts`
- Adds `parseRichObjectContent()` helper that detects JSON-encoded rich object payloads
- Extracts file links into `mediaUrls` (https/http only — rejects `file://`, `javascript:`, etc.)
- Resolves `{placeholder}` names for human-readable `displayText` (covers file shares, mentions, deck cards, polls, etc.)
- Uses `replaceAll` for placeholder substitution to handle duplicate occurrences
- Falls back to raw `content` for non-rich-object text — no behaviour change for regular messages

### `types.ts`
- Adds `mediaUrls?: string[]` to `NextcloudTalkInboundMessage`

### `inbound.ts`
- Passes `MediaUrls` into `finalizeInboundContext` (verified: already consumed natively as `string[]`)
- Relaxes the empty-body guard to allow media-only messages through (previously silently dropped)

## Tests

- `monitor.attachment.test.ts` — 7 unit tests: file share, text passthrough, malformed JSON fallback, name fallback, mention resolution, `file://` URL rejection, multi-file
- `monitor.live-integration.test.ts` — 5 integration tests using real HMAC-signed payloads against the webhook server

All 12 tests pass. TypeScript compiles clean (`tsc --noEmit`).

## Before / After

**Before:** Sharing a file in NC Talk → agent receives raw JSON string, never sees the attachment.

**After:** Sharing a file → agent receives `text = "filename.jpg"` and `mediaUrls = ["https://..."]` — can immediately process the image via vision without any manual workaround.

target.title: fix(gateway): include MCP tools in effective inventory
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the Agent Tools dashboard / `tools.effective` inventory originally reported only static OpenClaw tools, so configured bundled MCP tools could be usable at runtime but absent from the session-effective inventory.
- Solution: split the Gateway surface so `tools.effective` is a read-only peek projection with explicit MCP notices, and add `tools.effective.refresh` as an `operator.write` method that performs live MCP discovery/materialization on demand.
- What changed: effective inventory projection supports `source: "mcp"`; session MCP runtimes expose peek-only catalog metadata; the Gateway read path no longer cold-starts MCP transports; the refresh path materializes MCP tools through the existing runtime/materializer/final-policy path; Control UI adds an explicit **Refresh Available Tools** action; SDK/docs/protocol/tests cover the new split.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider transport changes, no Tool Search/Code Mode raw provider-body changes, no auto-reply `/tools` command changes, no install-package-dir changes, no broad shared-runtime refactor, and no claim that #80909 or the reporter-specific SSE raw provider-body path is fixed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #: N/A (intentionally not closing #76724; this keeps the reporter-specific SSE/runtime-provider-body path out of scope)
- Related / Refs #76724
- Related #70876
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

#76724 reports MCP handshake success while the Agent Tools dashboard stays stuck at the pre-MCP tool count and does not list MCP tools. Runtime code can materialize bundled MCP tools for model runs, but the dashboard inventory used a separate static inventory path. The initial patch made `tools.effective` perform live MCP materialization; after review of the read/security boundary, this update keeps `tools.effective` read-only and moves live discovery behind an explicit refresh method.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `tools.effective` should not cold-start/list MCP transports from a read-scoped dashboard load, while the dashboard must still have an explicit path to discover configured MCP tools and then show the warm catalog.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Gateway from this branch at `3c7a4e67b6`, QA mock OpenAI provider, real stdio MCP server created by the proof harness, Tool Search disabled, Code Mode disabled.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT=/root/repos/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective \
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_HEAD=3c7a4e67b6 \
OPENCLAW_UPSTREAM_COMMIT=110042d840 \
OPENCLAW_RPC_PROOF_SUMMARY=/tmp/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective-d-proof-summary.json \
node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective-d-proof.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

Copied live Gateway RPC harness output from this branch:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "proof": "D read/refresh split: cold read peek-only, explicit refresh materializes MCP, warm read peeks catalog",
  "upstreamCommit": "110042d840",
  "localHead": "3c7a4e67b6",
  "cold": {
    "noticeIds": ["mcp-not-yet-connected"],
    "containsMcpTarget": false,
    "runtimeCreated": false,
    "groupSummaries": [
      { "id": "core", "source": "core", "toolCount": 18 },
      { "id": "plugin", "source": "plugin", "toolCount": 2 }
    ]
  },
  "refreshed": {
    "noticeIds": [],
    "containsMcpTarget": true,
    "matchingIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"],
    "runtimeCreated": true,
    "catalogToolNames": ["probe_tool"],
    "groupSummaries": [
      { "id": "core", "source": "core", "toolCount": 18 },
      { "id": "plugin", "source": "plugin", "toolCount": 2 },
      { "id": "mcp", "source": "mcp", "toolCount": 1, "sampleIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"] }
    ]
  },
  "warm": {
    "noticeIds": [],
    "containsMcpTarget": true,
    "matchingIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"],
    "groupSummaries": [
      { "id": "core", "source": "core", "toolCount": 18 },
      { "id": "plugin", "source": "plugin", "toolCount": 2 },
      { "id": "mcp", "source": "mcp", "toolCount": 1, "sampleIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"] }
    ]
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: a cold `tools.effective` read returns only core/plugin groups plus `mcp-not-yet-connected` and does not create a session MCP runtime; explicit `tools.effective.refresh` materializes the configured stdio MCP server and returns `reproProbe__probe_tool`; the next `tools.effective` read shows the warm MCP group via peek without another cold materialization.
- What was not tested: the reporter's original Flask SSE server and release version `v2026.5.2`; Tool Search / Code Mode raw provider-body behavior; #80909; full cross-platform/browser E2E. This PR is scoped to the Gateway/dashboard effective-inventory boundary.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): pre-patch proof on current main returned 20 tools and omitted `reproProbe__probe_tool` from `tools.effective` while the runtime provider path could include it.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `tools.effective` called the static `resolveEffectiveToolInventory()` resolver, which builds from `createOpenClawCodingTools()` only. Bundled MCP tools are appended later in the model-run path through the session MCP runtime/materializer, so the dashboard inventory and runtime provider path diverged.
- Boundary correction in this update: live MCP discovery/listing can start configured stdio/remote transports, so it now lives behind explicit `operator.write` `tools.effective.refresh`; the read-scoped method only peeks at an existing matching-fingerprint runtime catalog and otherwise returns a notice.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered core/plugin/channel grouping but did not cover bundled MCP catalog projection, cold-read side effects, refresh materialization, or stale MCP config fingerprints through the `tools.effective` handler/RPC path.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end-style Gateway RPC proof
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/tools-effective.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`
  - `packages/sdk/src/index.test.ts`
  - `packages/sdk/src/index.e2e.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/controllers/agents.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/views/agents-panels-tools-skills.browser.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: cold `tools.effective` does not create/list MCP and returns notices; warm read projects an existing same-fingerprint catalog; fingerprint mismatch returns refresh-needed notice instead of stale MCP tools; explicit `tools.effective.refresh` materializes MCP tools and applies final effective policy; UI uses read on normal load and refresh on explicit action.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug and security-boundary concern live at the seam between static inventory, session MCP runtime, Gateway method scope, SDK, and UI refresh behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none found for bundled MCP in `tools-effective` / `tools-effective-inventory` before this patch.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The Agent Tools panel now loads without implicitly starting configured MCP transports. If MCP servers are configured but not connected/listed yet, the panel shows an explanatory notice and a **Refresh Available Tools** action. Triggering refresh performs live discovery and lets subsequent reads show the warm MCP tools.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before this PR:
[tools.effective] -> [static createOpenClawCodingTools inventory] -> [core/plugin/channel only]

Updated design:
[tools.effective]         -> [static inventory + peek existing MCP catalog] -> [core/plugin/channel + notices or warm mcp]
[tools.effective.refresh] -> [session MCP runtime materialize] -> [final bundled-tool policy] -> [core/plugin/channel + mcp]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No new capability beyond existing configured MCP materialization; yes, a new explicit Gateway method is added.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? Yes, only from explicit `tools.effective.refresh` or existing run-lifecycle MCP materialization, not from cold `tools.effective` reads.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, refresh can start/list configured MCP transports; it does not invoke MCP tools.
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: live MCP listing can start a configured stdio server or connect to a configured remote endpoint, so this update moves that side effect out of `operator.read` and into explicit `operator.write` `tools.effective.refresh`. The read path only peeks an existing runtime catalog with a matching config fingerprint and returns notices otherwise. Refresh reuses the existing session MCP runtime/materializer/final effective policy path and releases the materializer lease.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local dev environment
- Runtime/container: local Node via `node --import tsx`
- Model/provider: QA mock OpenAI provider, `mock-openai/gpt-5.5`
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WebSocket RPC `sessions.create`, `tools.effective`, `tools.effective.refresh`, then `tools.effective`
- Relevant config (redacted): `tools.profile: "coding"`, `tools.toolSearch: false`, `tools.codeMode.enabled: false`, `mcp.servers.reproProbe` stdio server command `node <tmp>/dummy-mcp-server.mjs`

### Steps

1. Start QA mock provider and local Gateway from this branch.
2. Create a session with `sessions.create`.
3. Call cold `tools.effective` and assert no MCP runtime was created and `reproProbe__probe_tool` is absent.
4. Call `tools.effective.refresh` and assert `reproProbe__probe_tool` appears in an `mcp` source group.
5. Call warm `tools.effective` and assert the MCP group is projected via peek.

### Expected

- Cold read: no MCP cold-start/listing; notice returned.
- Refresh: MCP listed/materialized through explicit write-scoped method.
- Warm read: MCP group visible through existing catalog peek.

### Actual

- Cold read: `runtimeCreated: false`, `containsMcpTarget: false`, `noticeIds: ["mcp-not-yet-connected"]`.
- Refresh: `runtimeCreated: true`, `matchingIds: ["reproProbe__probe_tool"]`, `catalogToolNames: ["probe_tool"]`.
- Warm read: `containsMcpTarget: true`, no stale not-connected notice.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main HEAD` → exit 0
  - `git diff --exit-code $(git merge-base upstream/main HEAD)..HEAD -- CHANGELOG.md` → no contributor-authored changelog entry remains
  - `pnpm check:test-types` → passed
  - `pnpm lint --threads=8` → passed
  - `pnpm protocol:check` → passed

  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts packages/sdk/src/index.e2e.test.ts` → 1 file passed; 4 passed / 1 skipped
  - `cd ui && pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/ui/controllers/agents.test.ts src/ui/views/agents-panels-tools-skills.browser.test.ts src/ui/views/agents.test.ts` → 3 files / 28 tests passed

- Edge cases checked:
  - final effective policy still filters materialized MCP tools
  - stale MCP config fingerprints produce refresh-needed behavior instead of serving stale MCP tools
  - read path does not call `getOrCreateSessionMcpRuntime()` on cold load

- What you did **not** verify:

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Config/env changes? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: users may expect MCP tools to appear immediately on panel load.
  - Mitigation: the panel now displays MCP notices and an explicit refresh action; warm catalogs still appear in read-only inventory.
- Risk: refresh can start configured MCP transports.
  - Mitigation: refresh is explicit and registered as `operator.write`; cold `operator.read` inventory no longer starts or lists MCP transports.
- Risk: dashboard/policy projection may still differ from a live inbound-message run when the UI lacks sender identity.
  - Mitigation: do not accept caller-supplied auth/delivery context; use server-derived session fields and final policy only.
- Risk: overlap with #70876.
  - Mitigation: this PR is narrower and avoids unrelated auto-reply/install-package-dir changes; related work is explicitly linked.

target.title: feat: add configurable timestamps to TUI chat messages
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #48708 — adds an option to display timestamps on each message in the TUI chat interface.

## Usage

Enable in config:
```json
{
  "tui": {
    "showTimestamps": true
  }
}
```

Messages then display as:
```
[11:32] system message here
[11:32]
user message here
[11:33] assistant response here
```

Disabled by default to preserve the current clean look.

## Changes

- `src/tui/components/chat-log.ts`: Added `showTimestamps` property and `formatTimestamp()` helper. `addUser`, `addSystem`, and `startAssistant` prepend `[HH:MM]` when enabled.
- `src/tui/tui.ts`: Read `tui.showTimestamps` from config and pass to ChatLog.

target.title: fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse t…
target.body: # fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse them during onboarding ## Summary - **Problem**: DeepInfra plugin pinned the chat catalog to 7 hardcoded manifest entries (a wizard-vs-discovery guard short-circuited discovery once the auth wizard seeded models), had no live discovery seam for image-gen / video-gen, and silently dropped Anthropic `cache_control` markers when proxying `anthropic/*` models — prompt caching was advertised eligible but never fired. - **Solution**: Wire DeepInfra's `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` (the agent-tagged projection now shipping in the backend) through a per-surface helper that buckets responses by short-alias tag (`chat`/`vlm`/`embed`/`image-gen`/`video-gen`/`tts`/`stt`). Register live image-gen and video-gen catalogs via `registerModelCatalogProvider` (OpenRouter pattern). Replace the OpenRouter-only Anthropic cache wrapper with a deepinfra-local one gated on the model id. - **What changed**: new `surface-model-catalogs.ts` (live image-gen + video-gen catalog providers + `resolveModelCapabilities`); new `cache-wrapper.ts` (Anthropic ephemeral cache marker injection for `anthropic/*` on DeepInfra); refactored `provider-models.ts` (per-surface bucketed discovery, API-key gated, 5-min cache, sync static fallback); media-provider builders now accept the per-surface catalog; `index.ts` feeds all media surfaces from one source + registers the new catalog providers; `media-models.ts` `DEFAULT_*` constants demoted to `*_FALLBACK_MODELS`; TTS default bumped to `Qwen/Qwen3-TTS`. - **Scope boundary**: No edits outside `extensions/deepinfra/`. No SDK changes (`src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts` is byte-identical to `main`). TTS/STT/VLM/embed surfaces still use static fallback only — extending `UnifiedModelCatalogKind` to cover them is a separate SDK PR. ## Motivation DeepInfra ships hundreds of models, but openclaw only surfaced 7 hardcoded chat IDs — every new model needed a code change here. The DeepInfra backend now emits surface-tagged responses on `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta`, so the plugin can map tags → surfaces dynamically. Anthropic prompt caching on DeepInfra was also silently broken: the runner advertised eligibility but the OpenRouter-only stream wrapper short-circuited on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so `cache_control` markers never went out. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix ## Scope - [x] Integrations ## Linked Issue/PR - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof - **Environment**: dev workstation + hosted instance (Ubuntu 24.04, `openclaw.service` via systemd, Node 22, `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` configured). Same matrix on both. - **Steps after this patch**: ```bash pnpm build pnpm test extensions/deepinfra rm -rf ~/.openclaw-dev DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability model providers | grep deepinfra DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability model list | grep '"provider":"deepinfra"' | wc -l DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability image providers | grep deepinfra DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability audio providers | grep deepinfra ``` - **Evidence after fix**: - `capability model providers` → `{"provider":"deepinfra","count":88,"defaults":["anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5","anthropic/claude-opus-4-7","anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"],"available":true,...}` (was: 7 on `main`). - `capability model list | grep '"provider":"deepinfra"' | wc -l` → 88 (was: 7). - `capability image providers` → deepinfra row with `FLUX-1-schnell` default + fallback allowlist. - `capability audio providers` → `{"id":"deepinfra","configured":true,"capabilities":["image","audio"],"defaultModels":{"image":"moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5","audio":"openai/whisper-large-v3-turbo"}}`. - `pnpm test extensions/deepinfra` → 59/59. - `pnpm check:changed` → exit 0 (one pre-existing unrelated lint failure in `scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs:398`, last touched in `85f9276624`). - **Not tested**: end-to-end TTS/STT audio round-trip and a real image/video-gen request against the live backend. The runtime providers register correctly and discovery returns the right shape; downstream inference was not exercised. ## Root Cause - (a) `augmentModelCatalog` short-circuited with `existingDeepInfraCount > DEEPINFRA_MODEL_CATALOG.length`, skipping discovery whenever the auth wizard had seeded entries first. - (b) Media surfaces had no `registerModelCatalogProvider` wiring. - (c) `wrapStreamFn` reused `createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper`, which short-circuits on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so Anthropic ephemeral cache markers never reached the payload for DeepInfra-routed Anthropic models even though `isCacheTtlEligible` returned `true`. **Missing guardrail**: no test asserted that `wrapStreamFn` applied `cache_control` markers for `anthropic/*` ids; no test asserted that the augmented catalog grew past the manifest size on successful discovery. ## Regression Test Plan - **Coverage level**: [x] Unit test [x] Seam / integration test - **Target tests**: - `extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts` (new) — asserts `cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }` markers are applied for `anthropic/*` ids and skipped otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/surface-model-catalogs.test.ts` (new) — asserts the live catalog providers return `null` without a key, return `null` on empty surface buckets, and project entries with the correct shape otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts` (extended) — per-surface bucketing + manifest fallback path. - `extensions/deepinfra/index.test.ts` (extended) — augmentModelCatalog merges discovered + wizard-seeded entries instead of suppressing one. - extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts (4 tests) exercises createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper against anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 & anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 and asserts cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" } is injected into the system message under both string-content and array-content shapes; negative test confirms non-anthropic ids pass through untouched. - extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts (3 new cases) mocks openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.isProviderApiKeyConfigured and asserts hasDeepInfraApiKey({ env: {}, agentDir }) falls back to the auth-profile store with the correct provider/agentDir args, short-circuits on env var, and returns false when both env and store are empty. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - **Chat catalogue**: with `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` set, OpenClaw surfaces the full DeepInfra catalogue (88 chat + 38 vlm today across `anthropic/`, `ByteDance/`, `deepseek-ai/`, `google/`, `meta-llama/`, `Qwen/`, `zai-org/`, …). Without a key, the curated 7-model manifest fallback is served. - **Image-gen + video-gen**: a live `registerModelCatalogProvider` now refreshes per-kind catalogs from DeepInfra when a key is configured. Without a key, the curated static fallback is served. - **TTS default**: `hexgrad/Kokoro-82M` → `Qwen/Qwen3-TTS`. Users on the implicit default will now hit Qwen3-TTS; Kokoro stays in the fallback list and can be selected explicitly. - **Onboarding picker**: discovered models are no longer hidden behind wizard-seeded entries. - **Anthropic prompt caching** actually fires on DeepInfra-routed `anthropic/*` requests. Multi-turn conversations should show non-zero `cache_creation_input_tokens` on turn 1 and `cache_read_input_tokens` on turn 2+. - Backward compatible; no config/env/migration changes. ## Diagram ```text Before: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds 7 manifest entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: count > MODEL_CATALOG.length? -> skip discovery] -> [picker shows 7 models] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper -> provider !== "openrouter" -> short-circuit] -> [payload sent WITHOUT cache_control markers, prompt cache never fires] After: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: always run discovery] -> [discoverDeepInfraSurfaces: GET /v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta] -> [bucket by surface alias tag] -> [picker shows 88 chat + 38 vlm + ...] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper: model.id starts with "anthropic/" -> apply patch] -> [payload sent WITH cache_control: {type: "ephemeral"}, prompt cache fires] ``` ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **Yes** — one read-only GET to `https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` per process per 5 minutes, gated on `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` being configured. The endpoint is unauthenticated; the gate is a UX choice (pre-auth flows show the static fallback, no useless network traffic). No PII or secrets in the request. - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** ## Human Verification (Observed Result) ran `pnpm dev --dev onboard` with provider deepinfra and successfully loaded all discovery models: Tested various `pnpm dev --dev capability ` commands (see section "Real behavior proof") ## Risks and Mitigations - **Risk**: production DeepInfra backend hasn't (yet) tagged any `video-gen` model with the OPENAI tag, so the `video-gen` surface bucket from live discovery is empty. **Mitigation**: the live catalog seam returns `null` on empty buckets so the static `DEEPINFRA_VIDEO_FALLBACK_MODELS` list wins. Once the backend tags T2V models, the live catalog takes over automatically — no deepclaw redeploy needed. - **Risk**: TTS default change (`Kokoro-82M` → `Qwen3-TTS`) could surprise users on the implicit default. **Mitigation**: Kokoro stays in the fallback list and can be selected explicitly; documented in CHANGELOG.

target.title: ACP: add gateway-owned node-backed runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw did not yet have a complete **node-backed ACP runtime** where the gateway owns the durable state and a paired node acts only as the execution worker.
- Why it matters: without that split, restart/replay/reconnect behavior is too dependent on the live process, which makes lease fencing, terminal resolution, and outward delivery recovery harder to reason about and prove.
- What this PR adds:
  - a durable ACP gateway store for sessions, runs, accepted worker events, leases, terminal state, replay targets, and projector checkpoints
  - gateway ingestion of ACP worker events coming back from paired nodes
  - a real node-host ACP worker bridge and gateway/node command plumbing
  - a durable ACP reply projection/replay path so restart recovery can resume from gateway state instead of only from the live iterator
  - startup recovery for incomplete ACP node runs
  - restart/reconnect/cancel/close race hardening with focused restart/replay test coverage
  - architecture / protocol / verification docs for the new backend
- Landing-only follow-up: after rebasing onto current `main`, this PR also includes a few **test-only** isolation fixes in Feishu / Google / Google Chat extension tests that were needed to make the landing branch green. Those are not ACP runtime behavior changes.

## What changed by area

### 1) Durable ACP gateway state

New ACP store/state plumbing under `src/acp/store/**`:

- persists ACP sessions and runs
- persists accepted worker events
- tracks leases / lease epochs
- resolves canonical terminal state on the gateway
- stores replay targets and projector checkpoints separately from the runtime cursor

This is the foundation that lets the gateway stay authoritative even when execution happens on a paired node.

### 2) Gateway ↔ node ACP worker path

Main implementation areas:

- `src/gateway/**`
- `src/infra/node-commands.ts`
- `src/node-host/**`

This adds the node-backed ACP execution path:

- gateway can start/control ACP work on a paired node
- node-host can run the ACP worker role
- worker events stream back to the gateway over the existing node transport
- gateway remains the source of truth for run state and terminal resolution

### 3) Durable reply projection and replay

Main implementation areas:

- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-replay.ts`

This PR moves ACP outward delivery onto a durable replayable path:

- reply targets are persisted per run
- projector checkpoints are advanced only after successful delivery
- restart recovery can replay the unfinished suffix from durable state
- reconnect and duplicate accepted events do not re-deliver already completed outward effects

### 4) Startup recovery / reconnect handling

Main implementation areas:

- `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-node-events.ts`
- `src/acp/runtime/acp-node.ts`

This adds the gateway-side recovery flow needed to:

- resume incomplete projection after restart
- keep reconnect handling deterministic
- prevent stale-worker wins
- preserve canonical terminal behavior across restart/replay windows

### 5) Test harnesses and regression coverage

Main test additions/expansions:

- `src/acp/store/store.restart.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.replay.test.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.test.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-node-events.acp.test.ts`
- `src/node-host/invoke-acp.test.ts`
- `src/acp/test-harness/restart-harness.ts`

These cover the core failure windows this backend needs to survive:

- restart after accepted events but before replay checkpoint advancement
- restart after terminal persistence but before final outward convergence
- reconnect replay of already accepted events
- cancel / complete / close race handling
- restart recovery with a real memory cut instead of reusing in-process objects

### 6) Docs included in this PR

Main docs:

- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime-protocol.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime-verification.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-runtime-program.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-slice4-summary.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-slice4-final-judge.md`

These are included because this was developed as a staged architecture / verification effort and the final accepted behavior is documented there.

## Explicit scope boundaries / v1 limitations

This PR does **not**:

- turn nodes into mini-gateways
- add automatic cross-node continuation for an in-flight run
- claim durable resumed tool-message edit support after restart

Current conservative post-restart tool-update behavior remains:

- `append_only_after_restart`

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: ACP node runtime architecture / protocol / verification docs under `docs/experiments/plans/`
- Related: landing-only extension test isolation fixes after rebase onto current `main`

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- OpenClaw now has a first-class ACP node-backed runtime path where the gateway owns durable ACP truth and paired nodes execute the worker role.
- Gateway restart/recovery can resume incomplete ACP outward projection from durable state.
- Duplicate accepted worker-event replay on reconnect is hardened so it does not duplicate outward delivery.
- Post-restart tool updates intentionally follow the documented conservative v1 rule: `append_only_after_restart`.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes
- Data access scope changed? Yes

### Security / trust-boundary notes

- Paired nodes can now advertise ACP worker capability and receive ACP runtime commands over the existing gateway↔node trust boundary.
- Gateway↔node websocket traffic now carries ACP worker control / event flows in addition to existing node commands.
- Gateway now persists ACP runtime state needed for durable replay/recovery.
- The design mitigates stale-worker / duplicate-delivery risks via lease epochs, canonical terminal resolution, durable replay checkpoints, and reconnect/replay handling on the gateway.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (landing worktree on vm1) + GitHub Actions CI
- Runtime/container: Node / pnpm; Bun build path also checked
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): ACP gateway↔node runtime path
- Relevant config (redacted): ACP runtime restart/replay harnesses and paired-node fixtures

### Verified locally

- ACP targeted verification gate:
  - `pnpm test -- src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.replay.test.ts src/acp/store/store.test.ts src/acp/store/store.restart.test.ts src/acp/store/gateway-events.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.acp.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-acp.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/nodes.acp.test.ts`
- TypeScript guardrail:
  - `pnpm tsgo`
- Landing-only extension test fixes:
  - `pnpm test:extensions`

### CI / build notes

- `pnpm build` still fails only with the pre-existing Bun wrapper issue:
  - `Cannot find module './cjs/index.cjs' from ''`
- That build failure was already present and is not introduced by the ACP runtime changes in this PR.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the ACP-focused local gate listed above.
- Verified `pnpm tsgo` passes.
- Verified the rebased landing branch’s extension test-isolation fixes with local `pnpm test:extensions`.
- Verified the PR now includes the architecture / protocol / verification / final-judge docs for this backend.

What I did **not** verify:

- I did **not** get a clean `pnpm build` because of the pre-existing Bun wrapper failure noted above.
- I did **not** run a fresh manual end-to-end Discord/real-node interactive repro outside the checked-in restart/replay and node-host harnesses.

## Review Guide

If you want the shortest path through this PR, review in this order:

1. **Core architecture / intent**
   - `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime.md`
   - `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime-protocol.md`
2. **Gateway-owned durable state**
   - `src/acp/store/**`
3. **Node execution bridge**
   - `src/node-host/**`
   - `src/gateway/server-methods/nodes.ts`
   - `src/gateway/server-node-events.ts`
4. **Durable outward replay path**
   - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts`
   - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts`
   - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-replay.ts`
5. **Recovery and proofs**
   - `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.ts`
   - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.replay.test.ts`
   - `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.test.ts`
6. **Ignore as landing-only test fixes unless you are checking CI fallout**
   - `extensions/feishu/**`
   - `extensions/google/**`
   - `extensions/googlechat/**`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No explicit migration required
- Migration needed? No

Notes:

- This adds a new ACP runtime/backend path; it does not force existing deployments to switch to it immediately.
- Post-restart tool updates intentionally remain append-only in v1.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR or avoid selecting/using the `acp-node` runtime path until fixed.
- Files/config to restore: ACP runtime/store/gateway/node-host changes under `src/acp/**`, `src/node-host/**`, `src/gateway/**`, plus the accompanying docs if reverting wholesale.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: duplicate final delivery after restart, stale worker winning after reconnect, projector checkpoint drift, or replay targeting the wrong route.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: restart/replay correctness is subtle in any durable projection system.
  - Mitigation: this PR includes restart harnesses, startup recovery tests, reconnect/replay ordering tests, and explicit protocol/verification docs.
- Risk: the PR is large and spans multiple ACP runtime layers.
  - Mitigation: the review guide above splits it into durable store, node bridge, replay path, recovery path, and landing-only CI fixes.
- Risk: paired-node ACP execution expands the gateway↔node command surface.
  - Mitigation: the gateway remains authoritative for leases, terminal resolution, checkpoints, and replay instead of trusting node-local completion.

---

## AI Checklist

- [x] **AI-assisted** — agent-harness / Codex driven implementation, review, and landing follow-ups
- [x] **Testing degree** — focused local verification plus CI
- [x] **Understanding confirmed** — this PR adds the gateway-owned ACP node-backed runtime and includes a few landing-only extension test fixes after rebase
- [ ] Prompts/session logs — available on request

target.title: fix(plugins): resolve native plugin sdk aliases
target.body:
## Summary
- install a narrow native resolver for `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` and `@openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` when loading plugin modules
- keep native `createRequire()` SDK subpath resolution on built JS dist artifacts, even when the plugin loader prefers source aliases
- guard the resolver to parents under registered plugin roots so unrelated process `require()` calls still use Node's normal resolver
- add regression coverage for read-only dist trees, source/dist preference, and the parent-root guard

Refs #85123.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/plugin-sdk-native-resolver.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: built external plugin runtimes can resolve OpenClaw plugin SDK subpaths from plugin-local `createRequire()` calls without a plugin-local `node_modules/openclaw` shim, runtime writes to the OpenClaw package tree, or `NODE_PATH`.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout using a fake built OpenClaw package and fake built external plugin runtime under `src/plugins/plugin-sdk-native-resolver.test.ts`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/plugin-sdk-native-resolver.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: the regression test resolves `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-message` from the registered external plugin runtime, verifies the resolved file is `dist/plugin-sdk/channel-message.js` even when source files exist, verifies no `dist/extensions` alias tree is created in a read-only dist tree, and verifies an unrelated parent module cannot resolve the alias through this hook.
Observed result after fix: 1 test file passed, 3 tests passed.
What was not tested: no live third-party Mentat runtime; this PR intentionally covers the generic resolver half only and leaves channel behavior to the generic channel-message PR.

target.title: fix(thinking): let authoritative provider profiles override stale catalog reasoning=false
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84880. Subagent spawns and `/think` validation reject non-`off` thinking on canonical `openai/*` and `openai-codex/*` GPT-5 reasoning models when a stale catalog entry has `reasoning: false`. `resolveThinkingProfile` short-circuits to off-only on `reasoning === false` before consulting the provider plugin profile, so persisted catalogs that lag behind runtime augmentation force `Use one of: off.` even for models the provider knows support `high`/`xhigh`.

Introduces an `overridesCatalogReasoning?: boolean` flag on `ProviderThinkingProfile`. When a provider returns a profile with this flag set, the resolver treats the plugin as the canonical source and uses its level list ahead of the catalog opt-out. Providers opt in **per model**, so non-reasoning models (e.g. `openai/chat-latest`, `openai/gpt-5.3-chat-latest`) still honor catalog `reasoning: false`.

The OpenAI extension opts in for GPT-5 reasoning model refs and explicitly excludes non-reasoning chat-latest variants. The classifier is **suffix-aware**: any `*-chat-latest` model is catalog-authoritative unless it appears in the explicit `OPENAI_REASONING_CHAT_LATEST_MODEL_IDS = ["gpt-5.1-chat-latest", "gpt-5.2-chat-latest"]` opt-in list (matches bundled catalog reasoning flags in `extensions/openai/openclaw.plugin.json`).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `sessions_spawn(model="openai-codex/gpt-5.5", thinking="high"|"xhigh")` and `sessions_spawn(model="openai/gpt-5.4", thinking="high")` are rejected with `Thinking level "high" is not supported for openai/gpt-5.5. Use one of: off.` even though the canonical OpenAI/Codex catalog lists these models as reasoning-capable — see live v2026.5.20 repro logs in [issue #84880](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84880). The patch reorders `resolveThinkingProfile` so an authoritative provider profile (now flagged via `overridesCatalogReasoning`) wins over stale persisted catalog `reasoning: false` rows.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 25.5.0 (Darwin arm64), Node 24.13.0, pnpm 11.1.0; repository checked out at `fix/subagent-thinking-overrides-catalog-reasoning` head `69986fc5` rebased on `origin/main` `f483f59b`; runtime probe driving the patched modules end-to-end through the same `resolveOpenAIThinkingProfile` / `resolveOpenAICodexThinkingProfile` entry points that the live Gateway calls on every `sessions_spawn` validation in `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts:794` and `src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts:1172`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
git checkout fix/subagent-thinking-overrides-catalog-reasoning
node --import tsx probe-thinking.mjs
```

The probe is a small driver script that imports the patched `extensions/openai/thinking-policy.ts` and calls the classifier for each acceptance scenario from #84880 plus the chat-latest regression guards from the ClawSweeper review, then checks the resolved `levels[]` and `overridesCatalogReasoning` flag against expected values.

**Evidence after fix**: Live terminal output from `node --import tsx probe-thinking.mjs` after applying the patch — copied verbatim, no redaction needed since the probe data is synthetic:

```
=== Runtime probe: extensions/openai/thinking-policy.ts ===

PASS | [#84880 §1] openai/gpt-5.4 high
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | [#84880 §2] openai-codex/gpt-5.5 high
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | [#84880 §3] openai-codex/gpt-5.5 xhigh
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/gpt-5.5 minimal/low/medium/high/xhigh
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | [ClawSweeper P1] openai/chat-latest
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=false (want false)
PASS | [ClawSweeper P1] openai/gpt-5-chat-latest
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=false (want false)
PASS | [ClawSweeper P1] openai/gpt-5.3-chat-latest
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=false (want false)
PASS | openai/gpt-5.1-chat-latest (reasoning catalog row)
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/gpt-5.2-chat-latest (reasoning catalog row)
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/o3-mini
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/gpt-5-pro
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/gpt-4.1
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=false (want false)

Summary: all 12 probes PASS.
```

**Observed result after fix**: 12/12 probes pass. For the canonical GPT-5 reasoning model refs the patch flips `overridesCatalogReasoning` to `true` and surfaces the full `[off, minimal, low, medium, high]` level set (plus `xhigh` on the appropriate models), so a stale catalog row marked `reasoning: false` no longer triggers the off-only short-circuit. For non-reasoning chat-latest variants (`chat-latest`, `gpt-5-chat-latest`, `gpt-5.3-chat-latest`) and legacy non-reasoning models (`gpt-4.1`) the classifier returns `overridesCatalogReasoning=false`, so a stale catalog `reasoning: false` row still wins and the runtime keeps the off-only path documented by the catalog opt-out.

Cross-check against the bundled `extensions/openai/openclaw.plugin.json` shows the classifier verdict matches the catalog truth for every chat-latest variant in the bundle:

| Model | Catalog reasoning | Classifier verdict | Stale-row override |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gpt-5-chat-latest` | `false` | non-authoritative | catalog wins (off-only) |
| `gpt-5.1-chat-latest` | `true` | authoritative | overrides stale `false` |
| `gpt-5.2-chat-latest` | `true` | authoritative | overrides stale `false` |
| `gpt-5.3-chat-latest` | `false` | non-authoritative | catalog wins (off-only) |
| `chat-latest` | `false` | non-authoritative | catalog wins (off-only) |
| `gpt-5.5`, `gpt-5.4`, `gpt-5-codex`, `o3-mini`, … | `true` | authoritative | overrides stale `false` |
| `gpt-4.1`, `gpt-4o`, … | `false` | non-authoritative | catalog wins (off-only) |

**What was not tested**: A redacted `sessions_spawn` live run against a custom-built Gateway binary in my deployment — the probe exercises the same classifier and resolver entry points the live Gateway calls, but I did not build and install a custom Gateway binary into my OrbStack VM for this PR. Also out of scope: the Ollama Cloud variant raised by @SplyzerRB in #84880 where the subagent ignores explicit `thinking: "off"` — that path forwards `thinking` even when the spawn arg is `off` and is a separate runtime bug that does not share a code path with the OpenAI/Codex catalog-override issue this PR fixes.

## What changes

- **`src/plugins/provider-thinking.types.ts`** — add `overridesCatalogReasoning?: boolean` to `ProviderThinkingProfile`.
- **`src/auto-reply/thinking.ts`** — reorder `resolveThinkingProfile`: when the plugin profile is authoritative, it wins over catalog `reasoning: false`; otherwise the existing off-only short-circuit applies.
- **`extensions/openai/thinking-policy.ts`** — suffix-aware classifier for chat-latest variants; flags reasoning model refs as authoritative via `isOpenAIReasoningModelId`.
- **`docs/tools/thinking.md`** and **`docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md`** — document the new opt-in flag.
- **`docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`** — regenerated via the API baseline generator.

Additionally — **`fix(plugins): break plugin-registry-snapshot ↔ plugin-metadata-snapshot.types import cycle`** (`69986fc5`):
- Pre-existing madge cycle on main introduced by upstream `bf64de91`. Verified by reverting this PR's diff and re-running the architecture check on plain `origin/main` — cycle still reproduces.
- Extract `PluginRegistrySnapshotSource` to a new leaf module `src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.types.ts`; `plugin-registry-snapshot.ts` re-exports the same name so no downstream import changes. Happy to split into a separate PR if maintainers prefer.

## Notes

- Related to PR #84902 (NianJiuZst), which takes the same architectural direction. This PR is an independent attempt with additional regression-guard tests covering the `gpt-5.3-chat-latest` chat-latest variant raised by ClawSweeper review. Happy to defer to or close in favor of #84902 if maintainers prefer that thread.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: [token efficiency] subagent bootstrap files
target.body:
## TL;DR Sub-agents are task workers, not always full copies of the main agent. This PR lets users opt in to a smaller, exact bootstrap-file list for sub-agent sessions so delegated workers can avoid spending tokens on `SOUL.md`, `USER.md`, long runbooks, or customer context they do not need. This is backwards compatible: omitted config keeps today’s built-in sub-agent behavior, cron filtering is unchanged, and `SUBAGENTS.md` is only loaded when explicitly configured. Closes #85282. ## Why this makes sense - **Token efficiency**: every spawned sub-agent currently pays for inherited bootstrap context even when the controller already gives it a focused task. A dedicated `SUBAGENTS.md`, `AGENTS.md` only, or `[]` profile avoids repeating unrelated persona, identity, user, and memory-adjacent context across parallel workers. - **Least-privilege context**: sub-agents should receive the context required for their delegated task. This gives users a core config knob to keep sensitive main-agent context such as `SOUL.md`, `USER.md`, or customer runbooks out of worker sessions unless they intentionally opt in. - **Codex fits naturally**: Codex ACP sub-agent targets can be configured with `subagentBootstrapFiles: ["AGENTS.md"]`. Codex already operates inside the project/workspace model, and the controller prompt can pass the concrete files and instructions needed for the task, so duplicating the main agent’s full personality/user stack is usually wasteful. - **Model-agnostic by design**: the behavior is expressed through agent profiles, not string-matching model names. Non-Codex targets can use `SUBAGENTS.md`; Codex targets can use `AGENTS.md`; specialized workers can use no bootstrap files. ## Flow ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Main agent session"] --> B["Spawn sub-agent task"] B --> C{"subagentBootstrapFiles configured?"} C -- "No" --> D["Existing built-in sub-agent set
AGENTS + TOOLS + SOUL + IDENTITY + USER"] C -- "agents.defaults" --> E["Load exact default list
example: SUBAGENTS.md"] C -- "agents.list[] override" --> F["Load exact target-agent list
example: Codex gets AGENTS.md"] E --> G["Run bootstrap hooks"] F --> G D --> G G --> H{"Opted in?"} H -- "Yes" --> I["Re-apply exact configured list
excluded files stay excluded"] H -- "No" --> J["Preserve current filtering behavior"] I --> K["Sub-agent prompt"] J --> K ``` ## Compatibility contract - Omitted config preserves the current default sub-agent bootstrap set: `AGENTS.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, and `USER.md`. - `[]` is explicit and means no workspace bootstrap files for sub-agent sessions. - Per-agent config overrides `agents.defaults.subagentBootstrapFiles`. - The setting applies only to sub-agent session keys; cron bootstrap filtering remains unchanged. - `SUBAGENTS.md` is recognized for explicit root loading, but OpenClaw does not seed it during setup and does not include it in normal full-context loading. - For opted-in configs, the configured list is exact after hooks, so a hook cannot accidentally re-add excluded files such as `SOUL.md` or `USER.md`. ## What changed - Added `agents.defaults.subagentBootstrapFiles` and `agents.list[].subagentBootstrapFiles`. - Added first-class `SUBAGENTS.md` recognition for explicit loading only. - Threaded per-agent selection through bootstrap resolution, the embedded Pi preload path, and the system-prompt override path. - Preserved explicitly configured `BOOTSTRAP.md` when a sub-agent opts into it, including after setup completion. - Added resolver/schema/embedded-runner tests plus docs, schema labels/help, and generated config baseline updates. ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior or issue addressed**: Sub-agent sessions can now receive an exact, configured workspace bootstrap file list instead of always inheriting the built-in sub-agent profile set. This proves omitted config is unchanged, `SUBAGENTS.md` opt-in works, a Codex per-agent override receives only `AGENTS.md`, `[]` injects none, missing explicit files keep markers, explicit `BOOTSTRAP.md` remains honored after setup completion, hook-added excluded files stay out, hooks cannot erase the configured file, and lowercase legacy `memory.md` is not injected as configured `MEMORY.md`. - **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw source checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-subagent-bootstrap-files`, running the actual `src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts` resolver against real workspace files under `/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/openclaw-subagent-bootstrap-files`. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '

target.title: fix(gateway): ignore inherited launchd env for respawn
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: macOS gateway update respawn could treat inherited generic launchd env such as `XPC_SERVICE_NAME` as proof that the gateway itself is launchd-supervised.
- Solution: narrow Darwin respawn-supervisor detection to the OpenClaw-owned `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL`.
- What changed: generic launchd env vars remain in supervisor cleanup lists, but only `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL` now triggers Darwin launchd respawn supervision.
- What did NOT change: systemd and Windows Scheduled Task detection are unchanged.

## Motivation

- Fixes a gateway lifecycle failure where a GUI-spawned gateway can exit expecting launchd to restart it, even though only the parent app's inherited launchd/XPC environment was present.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #85224
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Darwin respawn-supervisor detection no longer classifies inherited generic launchd env (`XPC_SERVICE_NAME` / `LAUNCH_JOB_LABEL`) as OpenClaw gateway supervision, while the OpenClaw-owned launchd marker still works.
- Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout on `darwin`, branch `yuhao/fix-launchd-xpc-supervisor-85224`, commit `041c52846e611dd882328ae2a83bbd12538a7603`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx -e 'const { detectRespawnSupervisor } = await import("./src/infra/supervisor-markers.ts"); const cases = [{name:"inherited XPC only", env:{XPC_SERVICE_NAME:"ai.openclaw.mac"}}, {name:"generic launch job only", env:{LAUNCH_JOB_LABEL:"ai.openclaw.gateway"}}, {name:"openclaw launchd marker", env:{OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL:"ai.openclaw.gateway"}}]; console.log(`platform=${process.platform}`); for (const item of cases) console.log(`${item.name}: ${detectRespawnSupervisor(item.env, "darwin") ?? "null"}`);'
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

Terminal capture from the patched checkout:

```text
platform=darwin
inherited XPC only: null
generic launch job only: null
openclaw launchd marker: launchd
```

- Observed result after fix: inherited `XPC_SERVICE_NAME` and `LAUNCH_JOB_LABEL` return `null`, so update respawn will not take the supervised-exit path from inherited parent launchd state. `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL` still returns `launchd` for real OpenClaw LaunchAgent services.
- What was not tested: I did not run a full GUI-triggered update/restart against a live installed LaunchAgent; the proof validates the production supervisor detector behavior in the local macOS source checkout.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The previous implementation used all launchd hints for Darwin detection, so `XPC_SERVICE_NAME` alone matched the launchd hint list and returned `launchd`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `detectRespawnSupervisor` treated generic launchd/XPC environment variables as evidence that the current gateway process was launchd-supervised.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no regression test for a gateway child process inheriting `XPC_SERVICE_NAME` from a launchd-managed parent app.
- Contributing context (if known): Generated OpenClaw service environments already set the more specific `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL`, which is the safer marker for respawn supervision.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/infra/supervisor-markers.test.ts`, `src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts`, `src/daemon/service-env.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: inherited macOS `XPC_SERVICE_NAME` and generic launch job env do not trigger supervised restart; OpenClaw-owned launchd labels still do.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in the supervisor detector and update respawn branch selection, so source-level tests cover the exact boundary without requiring a live 30-second restart cycle.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing launchd-positive tests covered real markers; this PR adds the inherited-env negative cases.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

macOS gateways spawned from a launchd-managed parent no longer exit under the false assumption that launchd will restart them unless OpenClaw's own launchd service marker is present.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
GUI parent launchd env -> gateway child sees XPC_SERVICE_NAME -> supervised exit -> no gateway restart

After:
GUI parent launchd env -> gateway child sees XPC_SERVICE_NAME -> unmanaged update respawn path -> gateway returns
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (`darwin`)
- Runtime/container: local Node/tsx source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `XPC_SERVICE_NAME=ai.openclaw.mac`, `LAUNCH_JOB_LABEL=ai.openclaw.gateway`, `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL=ai.openclaw.gateway`

### Steps

1. Run the terminal capture command in the Real behavior proof section.
2. Run the regression and changed-check commands listed below.

### Expected

- Generic inherited launchd env returns `null`.
- `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL` returns `launchd`.

### Actual

- The patched checkout produced the terminal output shown above.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts src/infra/supervisor-markers.test.ts src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts src/daemon/service-env.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(pi-runner): flush block replies after compaction retry
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #47335.
- Flushes the block reply pipeline again after the compaction retry wait resolves, so retry-generated block replies cannot still be draining when the attempt returns.
- Skips the extra drain on aggregate timeout to preserve the existing timeout/slow-channel behavior.

## Verification
- `corepack pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-compaction-retry-delivery-proof.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "flushes block replies again after compaction retry wait resolves"`
- `pnpm check:architecture`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/compaction-retry-aggregate-timeout.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.splits-long-single-line-fenced-blocks-reopen.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.waits-multiple-compaction-retries-before-resolving.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.test-support.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Compaction retry can resolve the runner wait before retry-generated block replies finish draining, causing transcript-visible replies to be dropped before channel delivery.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at commit `70f45ff870e43fbb939c680b74821015ecdccce6`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `e2f82d4d30bbebbf89bdbb94b3e58c7aa0185151` on macOS, with dependencies installed from `pnpm-lock.yaml`.

The log-backed proof uses the real `createBlockReplyPipeline().flush()` delivery barrier and the real `waitForCompactionRetryWithAggregateTimeout()` helper. The local channel adapter deliberately delays send completion so the retry wait / delivery ordering is visible without external provider credentials.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `corepack pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-compaction-retry-delivery-proof.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "flushes block replies again after compaction retry wait resolves"`
- `pnpm check:architecture`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/compaction-retry-aggregate-timeout.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.splits-long-single-line-fenced-blocks-reopen.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.waits-multiple-compaction-retries-before-resolving.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.test-support.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix:
```txt
pre-wait-flush-start
pre-wait-flush-complete
retry-wait-start
retry-generated-block-enqueued
channel-send-start:retry reply delivered after compaction wait
retry-wait-resolved
aggregate-wait-result:{"timedOut":false}
delivered-before-post-wait-flush:none
delivery-in-flight-before-post-wait-flush-completes:yes
channel-send-complete:retry reply delivered after compaction wait
post-wait-flush-complete:retry reply delivered after compaction wait
```

- Rebased head: `70f45ff870e43fbb939c680b74821015ecdccce6` on `origin/main` `e2f82d4d30bbebbf89bdbb94b3e58c7aa0185151`.
- Target regression after rebase: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 2 passed | 94 skipped (96)`.
- Architecture after rebase: `Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).`, `Madge import cycle check: 0 cycle(s).`, `deprecated API usage guard passed`, `deprecated JSDoc guard passed`.
- Runner/lifecycle/compaction selection: `Test Files 6 passed (6)`, `Tests 166 passed (166)`.
- Supplemental payload/subscribe selection: `Test Files 5 passed (5)`, `Tests 62 passed (62)`.
- oxfmt: `All matched files use the correct format.`
- `git diff --check` passed with no output.

Observed result after fix: The retry wait returns with the retry reply still undelivered (`delivered-before-post-wait-flush:none`), then the second idempotent flush waits for the real block reply pipeline send chain to finish (`post-wait-flush-complete:retry reply delivered after compaction wait`). Aggregate-timeout fallback still avoids an extra drain.

What was not tested: A live high-context Discord/Telegram/other external-network compaction retry with real provider credentials was not run. The behavior proof is local and log-backed, using the real block reply pipeline flush and retry wait helper with a delayed local channel adapter.

## AI Assistance Disclosure
This PR was prepared with AI assistance. I reviewed the changed code path and verified the targeted tests above.

target.title: Fix/docs format check windows clean
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: pnpm format:docs:check uses git ls-files | xargs oxfmt which fails on Windows — xargs is unavailable and single-quoted globs in cmd.exe are treated as literals, silently matching no files and exiting 0 (false pass).
- Why it matters: Windows contributors cannot run or pass the docs formatting check locally, blocking PRs and giving misleading CI results on that platform.
- What changed: Replaced the shell pipeline with scripts/format-docs-check.cjs — a Node.js script using spawnSync with an argument array, bypassing the shell entirely. Updated package.json to invoke it.
- What did NOT change: Same files checked, same oxfmt --check tool, same exit codes. No runtime code, CI workflow logic, or docs content changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x ] Chore/infra
