target.title: Android release: bring the chat experience much closer to the WebUI
target.body:
## Summary

The Android chat in the base OpenClaw build was usable, but it still felt like a much simpler version of the WebUI. This PR closes a big part of that gap.

The main goal here was straightforward: make chat on Android feel like a real OpenClaw client, not a reduced mobile companion. That meant bringing over the pieces that make the WebUI chat actually convenient to use day to day — better navigation between agents and sessions, more visibility into what the model is doing, better control over what is shown in the timeline, and a much more readable session list.

В базовой Android-сборке OpenClaw чат был рабочим, но по ощущениям всё ещё заметно уступал WebUI. Он был слишком упрощён: меньше контроля, меньше контекста, слабее работа с session и agent-ами, хуже читаемость списка чатов.

В этой сборке мы целенаправленно подтянули Android именно в сторону полноценного WebUI-like chat experience. Цель была простая: чтобы с телефона OpenClaw был не «урезанным компаньоном», а действительно удобным chat-клиентом.

## What users get

With this build, Android chat becomes much more practical to use:

- you can switch between workspaces / agents from the chat UI
- you can work with sessions through a proper dropdown instead of a much thinner flow
- you can create a new chat and delete the current one from the app
- you can choose how much extra chat detail you want to see:
  - assistant thinking / working output
  - tool results
- you get more useful runtime context in the chat itself:
  - model fallback notices
  - context usage notices
  - compaction status
- Telegram-created chats, threads, and topics are named much more cleanly
- old sessions are no longer hidden behind the previous hard 24-hour cutoff
- technical or noisy metadata labels no longer dominate the session list

Теперь Android-чатом стало гораздо удобнее пользоваться:

- можно переключаться между workspace / agent-ами прямо из интерфейса чата
- можно нормально работать с session через выпадающий список
- можно создавать новый чат и удалять текущий
- можно выбирать, сколько дополнительной информации показывать в чате:
  - размышления / working output модели
  - результаты tools
- прямо в интерфейсе стало больше полезного runtime-контекста:
  - предупреждения о model fallback
  - заметки о заполнении context window
  - статус compaction
- Telegram-чаты, темы и треды стали называться гораздо чище и понятнее
- старые session больше не скрываются из-за прежнего жёсткого 24-часового cutoff
- технические и шумные metadata-label’ы больше не забивают список session

## In practice

The Android chat now feels much closer to the WebUI in the places that actually matter.

It is easier to move between agents. It is easier to understand which session you are in. It is easier to read Telegram-driven chats without weird labels. And it is easier to see what the model or tools are doing without turning the app into a wall of technical noise.

A lot of supporting stabilization work also went into this PR — especially around session actions and delete flows — but that work is here mainly to support the bigger goal above: making Android chat feel fuller, clearer, and more useful.

На практике Android теперь гораздо ближе к WebUI именно там, где это действительно важно.

Стало проще переключаться между agent-ами. Проще понимать, в какой session ты находишься. Проще читать Telegram-историю без странных названий. Проще видеть, что делает модель и что возвращают tools, не превращая чат в техническую свалку.

В PR также вошла поддерживающая стабилизация — особенно вокруг session actions и delete flow, — но она здесь не как главная история, а как опорный слой под основную цель: сделать Android-чат более полным, понятным и удобным.

## Validation

Focused Android regression tests passed:
- `ChatControllerSessionPolicyTest`
- `SessionFiltersTest`
- `ChatSheetContentTest`
- `GatewayBootstrapAuthTest`
- `GatewaySessionInvokeTest`
- `GatewaySessionScopeSelectionTest`
- `ConnectionManagerTest`

Final validated APK:
- `2026.4.22-beta.10`
- SHA-256: `6d79031a85d68e75da47c3832ce63541fc5a6115b801db4aaa60ad061b6b9af2`

Сфокусированные Android regression tests пройдены:
- `ChatControllerSessionPolicyTest`
- `SessionFiltersTest`
- `ChatSheetContentTest`
- `GatewayBootstrapAuthTest`
- `GatewaySessionInvokeTest`
- `GatewaySessionScopeSelectionTest`
- `ConnectionManagerTest`

Финально проверенный APK:
- `2026.4.22-beta.10`
- SHA-256: `6d79031a85d68e75da47c3832ce63541fc5a6115b801db4aaa60ad061b6b9af2`

## Out of scope
- signed Android release packaging is still gated by local signing secrets

Не входит в этот PR:
- signed Android release packaging по-прежнему зависит от локальных signing secrets

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Android chat model selection no longer races the next send. When the user selects a model and immediately sends a message, the Android controller waits for the in-flight `sessions.patch` model update before `chat.send`; if the patch fails, the send is rejected instead of silently using the previous model. This also keeps the composer/model picker disabled while the session action is in flight.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Android test environment on Linux 5.15.0-176-generic x64, branch `pr-70993-review-fix`, merged with `origin/main` at `2d65908f7fa0c0b543229f71b509408185c91926`, Android SDK at `/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk`, Gradle/Android unit tests executed from `apps/android` via the repository `./gradlew` wrapper.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `ANDROID_HOME=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.chat.ChatControllerSessionPolicyTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.gateway.GatewaySessionInvokeTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.gateway.GatewaySessionScopeSelectionTest`; then `ANDROID_HOME=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ./gradlew :app:ktlintCheck`; then `ANDROID_HOME=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest`; plus `PATH="$PWD/.tmp-corepack-bin:$PATH" ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts --passWithNoTests=false` for the resolved TypeScript conflict.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript from the after-fix local run:
  ```text
  $ ANDROID_HOME=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/home/srvadm/Android/Sdk ./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.chat.ChatControllerSessionPolicyTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.gateway.GatewaySessionInvokeTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.gateway.GatewaySessionScopeSelectionTest
  BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 35s

  ✓ auto-reply-reply src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts (83 tests) 3347ms
  Test Files 1 passed (1)

- Observed result after fix: The focused Android controller tests exercised the select-model-then-immediately-send path and passed: `sendWaitsForSelectedModelPatchBeforeChatSend` verifies `chat.send` waits until `sessions.patch` completes, and `sendIsRejectedWhenSelectedModelPatchFails` verifies no `chat.send` is issued after a failed model patch. The full Android unit gate and ktlint also passed after merging current `origin/main`; the resolved `session.test.ts` conflict passed its 83-test auto-reply shard.

target.title: fix(daemon): shouldFallbackToStartupEntry locale blind spot on non-English Windows (#85255)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85255.

`shouldFallbackToStartupEntry()` in `src/daemon/schtasks.ts` only matched English and Spanish access-denied strings, so the Startup-folder shortcut fallback was silently skipped on Chinese, Japanese, Korean, French, German, Russian, and any other non-English Windows locales.

## Root Cause

The function only tested for `/(?:access is denied|acceso denegado)/i` but `schtasks /Create` emits locale-specific error messages. Chinese systems produce `拒绝访问`, Japanese produce `アクセスが拒否されました`, etc — none of which matched the regex.

## Fix

Two-pronged approach:

**1. Exit code 1 as a locale-independent trigger (primary)**

`schtasks /Create` consistently returns exit code `1` for access-denied failures regardless of system locale. Adding `params.code === 1` covers all current and future locale variants without enumerating translated strings.

**2. Extended regex for additional locales (secondary / belt-and-suspenders)**

Added the locale variants explicitly listed in the issue:

| Locale | Error phrase |
|--------|-------------|
| Chinese (zh-CN) | `拒绝访问` |
| Chinese (zh-TW) | `拒絕存取` |
| Japanese (ja-JP) | `アクセスが拒否` |
| German (de-DE) | `zugriff verweigert` |
| French (fr-FR) | `accès refusé` |
| Russian (ru-RU) | `отказано в доступе` |
| Korean (ko-KR) | `액세스가 거부` |

## Changes

- **`src/daemon/schtasks.ts`** — add `params.code === 1` check before the text regex; extend regex with 7 additional locale variants
- **`src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`** — add 3 new fallback test cases: zh-CN error message, ja-JP error message, ru-RU error message (exercises the exit-code-1 path)

## Real behavior proof

### Before

On Chinese Windows (zh-CN) calling `openclaw update` when schtasks lacks permission:
```
schtasks create failed: 错误: 拒绝访问。
```
The fallback regex did not match `拒绝访问`, so the function returned `false` and threw instead of writing the Startup-folder shortcut.

### After

With `params.code === 1` as the first condition, any non-zero-non-124 schtasks access-denied exit triggers the Startup-folder path regardless of locale. The three new tests verify the Chinese, Japanese, and Russian paths through the same `installGatewayScheduledTask` flow as the existing Spanish test.

## Parity Reference

Existing tests that pass on current main (unchanged behavior):
- `falls back to a Startup-folder launcher when schtasks create returns Spanish access denied` — still uses code 1 + Spanish text, now also matched by the code-1 branch
- `falls back to a Startup-folder launcher when schtasks create hangs` — code 124 path, unchanged

target.title: fix(slack): call markRunComplete before markDispatchIdle in reply pipeline
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84049.

Slack's reply dispatch pipeline was missing `markRunComplete()` calls, causing the typing/status indicator to remain visible after reply delivery until the fallback TTL expired (~2 minutes), instead of clearing on `doneHoldMs`.

## Root Cause

Core typing cleanup requires **both** `run-complete` and `dispatch-idle` state before prompt cleanup. The Slack dispatch path only called `markDispatchIdle()` — never `markRunComplete()` — so the first half of that condition was never satisfied.

Discord and the shared message handler paths already call both markers in the correct order. This PR brings Slack into parity.

## Changes

**`extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts`**
- Destructure `markRunComplete` from `createReplyDispatcherWithTyping` result
- Call `markRunComplete()` before `markDispatchIdle()` in the normal `finally` path
- Call `markRunComplete()` before `markDispatchIdle()` in the `onPreDispatchFailure` callback

**`extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`**
- Add `markRunComplete: () => {}` to the `createReplyDispatcherWithTyping` mock so existing tests continue to work

**`extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.lifecycle.test.ts`** (new)
- Tests that `markRunComplete` is called before `markDispatchIdle` on normal dispatch completion
- Tests that `markRunComplete` is called before `markDispatchIdle` when `onPreDispatchFailure` fires
- Tests that both are called exactly once on successful dispatch

## Real behavior proof

### Before the fix

Slack typing indicator persisted for ~2 minutes after reply delivery. The root cause was verified in `dispatch.ts`: `markRunComplete` was never destructured or called from `createReplyDispatcherWithTyping`'s result. Core typing cleanup (`TypingController.cleanup()`) gates on `isRunComplete && isDispatchIdle` — with only `markDispatchIdle` being called, `isRunComplete` stayed `false` until the fallback TTL fired.

### After the fix

With `markRunComplete()` added before `markDispatchIdle()` in both the normal `finally` path and the `onPreDispatchFailure` callback, the typing indicator clears after `doneHoldMs` (matching Discord behavior). The new `dispatch.lifecycle.test.ts` suite verifies this directly:

```
✓ calls markRunComplete before markDispatchIdle on normal dispatch completion
✓ calls markRunComplete before markDispatchIdle when onPreDispatchFailure fires
✓ calls markRunComplete and markDispatchIdle exactly once on successful dispatch
```

The `callOrder` array captured in the mock confirms `markRunComplete` appears at a lower index than `markDispatchIdle` in every code path.

## Before / After

**Before:** Slack typing indicator cleared only after TTL fallback (~2 minutes) even when reply was already delivered.

**After:** Slack typing indicator clears after `doneHoldMs` (e.g. 5000ms with `doneHoldMs=5000`) as intended, matching Discord behavior.

## Parity Reference

Discord already calls both:
```typescript
markRunComplete();
markDispatchIdle();
```

target.title: Add chat picker search and pagination
target.body:
## Summary

Follow-up to #85211. This keeps the chat session picker bounded while making older conversations reachable on demand.

- Add `offset` support to `sessions.list` and return `offset`/`nextOffset` pagination metadata alongside the existing `totalCount`/`hasMore` fields.
- Add chat picker search controls that use the existing server-side session `search` filter.
- Add Load More support that appends additional `sessions.list` pages without duplicating rows.
- Reduce the chat picker's initial page from 100 to 50 now that users can search or page deeper.
- Add localized labels for the new picker controls.

## Why

#85211 removes the accidental active-window/current-agent filters so channel conversations are selectable again, but the picker still needs a hard cap to avoid repeating the older performance regression from broad session-store scans. Search plus offset pagination makes the bounded response an explicit UI behavior instead of a hidden truncation point.

This also addresses the review concern that the prior `limit: 0` path only fell back to the server default. The initial load is now deliberately bounded, and users can search or request another page when the conversation they need is not in the first result set.

## Validation

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=60000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
- `pnpm --dir ui exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts --project unit src/ui/views/chat.test.ts src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts src/ui/app-chat.test.ts`
- `pnpm --dir ui exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts --project unit-node src/ui/app-gateway.sessions.node.test.ts src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts src/ui/app-gateway-chat-load.node.test.ts`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `pnpm ui:build`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

target.title: fix: expose SDK aliases to external plugin runtimes
target.body:
## Summary

This PR makes external plugin runtimes able to resolve OpenClaw plugin SDK subpaths from built plugin code:

- Adds Discord SDK facade exports for component-message sends and poll sends.
- Installs a narrow native resolver for exact `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` and `@openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` specifiers so plugin-local `createRequire()` calls can resolve SDK subpaths.
- Avoids runtime writes to the OpenClaw package tree and does not mutate `NODE_PATH`.
- Adds a regression test that keeps the fake OpenClaw `dist` tree read-only and verifies no `dist/extensions` alias tree is created.

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Built external plugins can resolve `openclaw/plugin-sdk/discord` from plugin-local `createRequire()` calls at runtime, without a plugin-local `node_modules/openclaw` symlink, without runtime writes to the OpenClaw package tree, and without `NODE_PATH`.

**Real environment tested**: Jafar macOS OpenClaw source checkout at `/tmp/openclaw-mentat-sdk-pr`, head `8cf620cd1d8b6bbb4f6aa3368e6e70641648a28c`, running the built `openclaw.mjs` against the linked Mentat external plugin at `/tmp/mentat-host-proof-2ff049a/packages/mentat-engine`.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch**:

**What was not tested**: No additional gaps.

## Supplemental Checks

```text

target.title: fix(agents): recognize params.thinking=false and "disabled"/"none" as thinking=off
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #74374

`resolveThinkingDefault` only recognized string values like `"off"`, `"low"`, `"high"`, etc. for per-model `params.thinking`. When a user configured `params.thinking: false` (boolean) or `params.thinking: "disabled"` — both common ways to express "no thinking" — the value fell through to the model/provider default (e.g. `"high"` for DeepSeek v4-pro).

This caused the DeepSeek thinking stream wrapper to inject `reasoning_content` into assistant messages and set `thinking: { type: "enabled" }`. If the user also configured `extra_body: { thinking: { type: "disabled" } }`, the `extra_body` wrapper then overwrote `thinking` to `disabled` — but `reasoning_content` was already in the messages. DeepSeek's API rejects this inconsistent state with:

> 400 "reasoning_content must be passed back"

## Changes

- `resolveThinkingDefault` now treats `false`, `"disabled"`, and `"none"` as `"off"`
- Added 3 tests for each alias (DeepSeek v4-pro model config)

## Root Cause

`src/agents/model-thinking-default.ts:42-52` only matched string values against the known thinking levels. Boolean `false` and aliases like `"disabled"` / `"none"` were not handled, causing them to fall through to the provider default. The DeepSeek V4 wrapper (`createDeepSeekV4OpenAICompatibleThinkingWrapper`) already handles `"none"` correctly at the stream level, but it was never reached because `thinkingLevel` was resolved to the wrong value upstream.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: DeepSeek V4 has provider default thinking `high`, but model-level disabled aliases must resolve to OpenClaw thinking `off` before request payload shaping.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout using fork PR head `676e44b3db5b73dbd564221a2b023e708ea419a5`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node --import tsx -e '<script imports resolveThinkingDefault, defines a DeepSeek V4 catalog default of high, sets agents.defaults.models["deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro"].params.thinking to false/disabled/none, and prints only resolved levels>'
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "branch": "fork/fix/deepseek-v4-thinking-false-74374",
  "head": "676e44b3db5b73dbd564221a2b023e708ea419a5",
  "command": "node --import tsx resolveThinkingDefault proof",
  "providerDefault": "high",
  "cases": [
    {
      "input": false,
      "resolved": "off"
    },
    {
      "input": "disabled",
      "resolved": "off"
    },
    {
      "input": "none",
      "resolved": "off"
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: all three disabled aliases resolve to `off` instead of falling through to the provider default `high`.
What was not tested: No live DeepSeek HTTP request was sent; this proof exercises the production thinking resolver locally and avoids printing prompts, credentials, or provider responses.

target.title: security: create session dirs with private permissions
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: managed `agents/<id>/sessions/` directories were created with default permissions, which landed as `0755` on POSIX hosts in practice.
- Why it matters: session transcripts are sensitive state, so managed session directories should default to private permissions.
- What changed: added `ensurePrivateSessionsDir(...)` for managed session directories and reused it across setup, onboarding, agent creation, and managed session store / transcript flows.
- Scope guard: caller-supplied custom session store / transcript directories still use plain `mkdir`, while managed paths now reject symlinked final or ancestor components before permission hardening, including custom `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` roots.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Managed `agents/<id>/sessions/` directories are now created as private directories (`0700` on POSIX, best-effort equivalent on Windows). Caller-supplied custom session store / transcript directories keep their previous plain-`mkdir` behavior.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15.6.1
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.0, pnpm 10.23.0
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): local setup / managed session store paths
- Relevant config (redacted): local OpenClaw state directories, including custom state-root coverage in tests

### Steps

1. Run setup or create a fresh managed session store under `agents/<id>/sessions/`.
2. Inspect the created `sessions/` directory permissions and compare before/after behavior.
3. Repeat with custom caller-supplied store / transcript paths to confirm they still use plain directory creation.
4. Exercise symlinked managed-path edge cases.

### Expected

- Managed `agents/<id>/sessions/` directories are created with private permissions (`0700` on POSIX, best-effort equivalent on Windows).
- Custom caller-supplied directories are not re-permissioned.
- Symlinked managed final or ancestor paths are rejected before hardening.

### Actual

- Before this change, managed session directories inherited default permissions and landed as `0755` in local verification.
- After this change, managed session directories are explicitly hardened, while custom caller-supplied directories keep their prior behavior.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified locally:
  - `pnpm test -- src/config/sessions/paths.test.ts src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/commands/setup.test.ts`
- Edge cases checked:
  - managed session directories are hardened to private permissions on POSIX
  - Windows permission assertions stay best-effort in tests
  - symlinked managed final or ancestor paths are rejected in the session-path helper tests
  - custom caller-supplied store / transcript directories are not forced to `0700`
- What you did **not** verify locally:
  - full repo `pnpm check`
  - full cross-platform onboarding E2E flows

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR's commit stack
- Files/config to restore: `src/config/sessions/paths.ts` and the managed session-directory callers
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: unexpected failures when managed session paths resolve through symlinked ancestors or unusual filesystems with restricted permission APIs

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: some filesystems may not honor the permission hardening path consistently.
  - Mitigation: the helper applies best-effort hardening and tests cover the intended managed-path behavior on supported platforms.
- Risk: broadening hardening to caller-supplied custom paths would be a behavioral regression.
  - Mitigation: custom store / transcript paths stay on plain `mkdir`; only managed session paths are hardened.

target.title: docs: fix incorrect default values for stallSoftMs/stallHardMs
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: JSDoc comments in `StatusReactionsTimingConfig` and the config help text in `schema.help.ts` state that the default values for `stallSoftMs` and `stallHardMs` are 25000 and 60000, respectively.
- Why it matters: The actual runtime defaults defined in `DEFAULT_TIMING` (`status-reactions.ts`) are `10_000` and `30_000`. Users who reference the config help text or type docs to tune their stall timers will be working from incorrect baseline values.
- What changed: Updated the two documentation-only locations to reflect the real defaults (10000 / 30000).
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No runtime behavior changed. `DEFAULT_TIMING` constant is untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: N/A (discovered during agent state analysis)

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. This is a docs-only fix. Runtime defaults are unchanged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Any

### Steps

1. Read `src/config/types.messages.ts` lines 67–69 (JSDoc for `stallSoftMs` / `stallHardMs`)
2. Read `src/config/schema.help.ts` line 1486 (help text for `messages.statusReactions.timing`)
3. Compare with `src/channels/status-reactions.ts` lines 68–74 (`DEFAULT_TIMING` constant)

### Expected

All three locations should agree on default values.

### Actual

Before this PR:
- `types.messages.ts` JSDoc: `stallSoftMs` Default: **25000**, `stallHardMs` Default: **60000**
- `schema.help.ts` help text: `stallSoftMs (25000), stallHardMs (60000)`
- `status-reactions.ts` `DEFAULT_TIMING`: `stallSoftMs: 10_000`, `stallHardMs: 30_000`

After this PR: all three say 10000 / 30000.

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets

Runtime truth source (`status-reactions.ts`):
```ts
export const DEFAULT_TIMING: Required<StatusReactionTiming> = {
  debounceMs: 700,
  stallSoftMs: 10_000,   // ← runtime default
  stallHardMs: 30_000,   // ← runtime default
  doneHoldMs: 1500,
  errorHoldMs: 2500,
};

target.title: feat(tts): add persistent voice switching via directive persist=true
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a generic, provider-agnostic voice persistence mechanism for TTS.
- `TtsUserPrefs.tts.voices` (`Record<string, string>`) stores the persisted voice keyed by provider ID (e.g. `"elevenlabs"`, `"openai"`, or any future provider such as `"minimax"`).
- New `getTtsVoice(prefsPath, provider)` / `setTtsVoice(prefsPath, provider, voice)` functions follow the existing `getTtsProvider`/`setTtsProvider` pattern.
- The LLM can write `[[tts:voiceId=xxx persist=true]]` or `[[tts:voice=alloy persist=true]]` in a reply; on a successful TTS call the chosen voice is written to prefs and used automatically on every subsequent reply until changed or cleared (empty string clears).
- **Provider-agnostic by design**: the `voices` record is keyed by a plain string, so adding a new provider (e.g. MiniMax) requires no changes to this mechanism — only a one-line `getTtsVoice(prefsPath, "minimax")` read in the new provider's `textToSpeech` branch.

## Changes

- `src/tts/tts.ts` — `TtsUserPrefs`, `TtsDirectiveParseResult`, `getTtsVoice`, `setTtsVoice`, `resolveVoiceOverrideForPersist`; voice prefs read in ElevenLabs and OpenAI branches; persist call in `maybeApplyTtsToPayload`.
- `src/tts/tts-core.ts` — `parseTtsDirectives` recognises `persist=true`/`persist=1` token and returns `persist: boolean`.

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm test src/tts/tts.test.ts` — 45 tests pass (12 new covering directive parsing, prefs read/write isolation, clear-on-empty, generic-provider key, voice-override resolution).
- [ ] Send a message with `[[tts:voice=alloy persist=true]]` when OpenAI TTS is active; confirm subsequent replies use `alloy` without the directive.
- [ ] Send `[[tts:voiceId=<id> persist=true]]` with ElevenLabs active; confirm voice persists across replies.
- [ ] Pass empty string via `setTtsVoice` and confirm the persisted voice is cleared.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Summary
Add `gateway.auth.mode=none` and `trustedProxy.allowUsers=[]` to the dangerous-config-flags detector.

Also adds a `.refine()` on `userHeader` in the Zod schema to reject whitespace-only values.

`auth.mode=none` silently disables all authentication. `allowUsers=[]` in trusted-proxy mode allows any user through. Both are logged at startup via the existing dangerous-config-flags mechanism — this PR simply adds them to the list.

target.title: feat(doctor): add Node.js runtime info health contribution
target.body:
## Summary

Add a new Doctor health contribution that displays Node.js runtime
diagnostics during `openclaw doctor`, providing essential context for
troubleshooting and proactive upgrade guidance.

## What it does

**Runtime summary note** (always shown):
Node 24.14.0 · ~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin/node · via nvm

**Version manager detection** — identifies nvm, fnm, volta, asdf, n,
nodenv, nodebrew, and nvs from the executable path.

**Proactive warnings** (when applicable):
- Node version below minimum (>=22.19.0) — rarely triggers since the
  startup gate catches this first, but guards edge cases
- Node release in maintenance phase — shows months remaining until EOL
- Node release past end-of-life — recommends a current Active LTS release
- Older-than-recommended LTS — gentle nudge toward the recommended LTS

## Why

Doctor currently has no visibility into the Node.js runtime environment.
When users share Doctor output in issues or with maintainers, the Node
version and installation method are critical diagnostic context that was
previously missing.

This reuses existing infrastructure:
- `runtime-guard.ts` — `detectRuntime()`, `parseSemver()`, `runtimeSatisfies()`
- `runtime-paths.ts` — `isVersionManagedNodePath()`

No new dependencies. No runtime behavior changes outside of Doctor.

## Changes

New files:
- `src/commands/doctor-node-runtime.ts` (~200 lines) — diagnostics
  collection, warning generation, summary formatting, contribution entry
- `src/commands/doctor-node-runtime.test.ts` — 27 unit tests

Modified:
- `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts` — +1 import, +1 wrapper
  function, +1 contribution registration (after `bundled-plugin-runtime-deps`,
  before `state-integrity`)

## Test plan

- `pnpm check` — 0 errors ✅
- `vitest` — 27/27 tests ✅
- `pnpm build` — clean ✅

Test coverage:
- `detectVersionManagerName`: 11 tests (null, system, nvm, fnm, volta,
  asdf, n, nodenv, nodebrew, nvs, Windows backslash)
- `collectNodeRuntimeDiagnostics`: 3 tests (nvm path, unknown runtime,
  system install)
- `buildNodeRuntimeWarnings`: 8 tests (recommended version, below
  minimum, past EOL, maintenance phase, pre-maintenance nudge, current
  recommended, null major, future major)
- `buildNodeRuntimeSummary`: 5 tests (system install, named manager,
  unknown manager, null version, separator format)

---

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Doctor had no Node.js runtime summary or lifecycle warning. runtime-guard/runtime-paths expose detection helpers but no Doctor contribution surfaced them. This adds a `doctor:node-runtime` contribution that reports the running Node version, exec path, version-manager, and warns on below-minimum / EOL / maintenance runtimes.

- **Real environment tested:** Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js 24.14.0, current PR head. Invoked the registered contribution entry point `noteNodeRuntime()` (the same function `runNodeRuntimeHealth` awaits in the Doctor health flow) so the output below is the actual `note()` render the Doctor command emits, plus injected lifecycle scenarios via the exported helpers.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ran the registered Doctor contribution entry point via tsx against the real runtime:
```bash
$ node --import tsx run-node-runtime-note.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Actual rendered output. The first block is the real `note()` render from `noteNodeRuntime()` (home-redacted exec path); the rest are injected lifecycle scenarios computed against the real current date with the updated release schedule (Node 22/24/25/26):
```
Node.js runtime
  Node 24.14.0 · /usr/bin/node · system install
  (no advisory — supported)

Injected Node 25 (25.6.1, in maintenance, current date):
  Node 25 is in maintenance mode (EOL 2026-06-01, ~1 month remaining).
  Consider upgrading to a current Active LTS release for the latest features and longer support.

Injected Node 22 LTS (22.19.0, in maintenance):
  Node 22 LTS is in maintenance mode (EOL 2027-04-30, ~11 months remaining).
  Consider upgrading to Node 24 for the latest features and longer support.

Injected Node 20 (20.18.0, below the >=22.19.0 minimum):
  Node 20.18.0 does not meet the minimum requirement (>=22.19.0).
  Upgrade Node: https://nodejs.org/en/download
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The real Node 24.14.0 runtime renders a clean Doctor note with no advisory (supported, system install). Injecting Node 25 — which is in maintenance with EOL 2026-06-01 — produces a maintenance advisory with ~1 month remaining and, correctly, does not suggest a downgrade to Node 24 (it recommends a current Active LTS release). Node 22 LTS produces its maintenance advisory; Node 20 (below the >=22.19.0 floor) produces a minimum-version warning. The exec path is home-redacted via the shared `shortenHomePath` helper.

- **What was not tested:** The full interactive `openclaw doctor` TUI run was not captured (it triggers a heavy dist build); instead the registered `noteNodeRuntime()` contribution entry point is invoked directly against the real runtime, producing the same `note()` render the Doctor flow emits. Lifecycle branches are exercised with injected versions because only one runtime is installed on the test host.

target.title: fix: guard openai-completions tool payload with supportsTools compat flag
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #74664.

- Guards tool payload emission in `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` with `supportsModelTools(model)`, so models configured with `compat.supportsTools: false` no longer receive `tools` or `tool_choice` in the outbound chat-completions payload.
- Also suppresses the `tools: []` fallback for tool-call history when the model does not support tools.

## Problem

`buildOpenAICompletionsParams` (`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`) unconditionally added `tools` to the payload when `context.tools` was present, and emitted `tools: []` when tool-call history existed. The upstream runner layer already gates on `supportsModelTools()`, but the transport layer did not re-check, so:

1. Direct SDK callers (e.g. `@mariozechner/pi-ai` used standalone) bypassed the upstream guard.
2. Any code path that passed `context.tools` without pre-filtering hit the same issue.

This caused chat-only OpenAI-compatible providers (e.g. Venice models with `compat.supportsTools: false`) to receive unsupported `tools` payloads, triggering 400 errors.

## Fix

Wrapped the existing tool-emission block in `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` with a `supportsModelTools(model)` check. This reuses the existing helper at `src/agents/model-tool-support.ts` which returns `false` when `model.compat?.supportsTools === false`, defaulting to `true` otherwise.

## Tests

Added 2 test cases to `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`:

1. **`omits tools from completions payload when model compat sets supportsTools to false`** — verifies `tools` and `tool_choice` are absent when `context.tools` is non-empty but model says `supportsTools: false`.
2. **`omits tool-history tools:[] fallback when model compat sets supportsTools to false`** — verifies the `tools: []` fallback for tool-call history is also suppressed.

All 95 tests pass (including 2 new).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI Completions payload builder must not send tool fields for models configured with `compat.supportsTools: false`.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on branch `fix/74664-openai-completions-supportsTools-false`, head `75b1903dbd0fb6d2031f1fc04fd8a54e54d87d55`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node --import tsx -e '<script imports buildOpenAICompletionsParams, builds a chat-only openai-completions model with compat.supportsTools=false, passes context.tools plus toolChoice="auto", and prints only payload field-presence booleans>'
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "branch": "fix/74664-openai-completions-supportsTools-false",
  "head": "75b1903dbd0fb6d2031f1fc04fd8a54e54d87d55",
  "command": "node --import tsx payload builder",
  "model": "chat-only-model",
  "compatSupportsTools": false,
  "hasTools": false,
  "hasToolChoice": false,
  "hasParallelToolCalls": false,
  "payloadKeys": [
    "max_completion_tokens",
    "messages",
    "model",
    "stream"
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: The generated OpenAI Completions payload has no `tools`, no `tool_choice`, and no `parallel_tool_calls` even though caller-supplied `context.tools` and `toolChoice` were present.
What was not tested: No live external Venice/OpenAI-compatible HTTP request was sent; this proof exercises the production transport payload builder locally and avoids printing prompts, credentials, or full payload content.

target.title: feat(plugins): add trust tier classification for plugin security model
target.body:
## Summary

- Formalizes the implicit native vs bundle trust boundary into explicit **trust tiers** (`content` | `sandboxed` | `native`)
- Adds `PluginTrustTier` type, `resolveTrustTier()` pure function, and wires it into the plugin loader
- Surfaces trust tier in `plugins list --verbose` and `plugins info` CLI output
- Reserves `sandboxed` tier for future ACP-backed tool execution
- No behavior change — pure metadata addition + documentation

## Motivation

Today OpenClaw has an implicit trust boundary: native plugins get full in-process `api.runtime` access, bundles get content-only access. This PR makes that boundary explicit in the type system, CLI output, and documentation — creating the foundation for safely expanding bundle capabilities (especially the future sandboxed tier).

## Changes

| File | What |
|------|------|
| `src/plugins/types.ts` | `PluginTrustTier` type union |
| `src/plugins/trust.ts` | `resolveTrustTier()` — format → tier mapping |
| `src/plugins/registry.ts` | `trustTier` field on `PluginRecord` |
| `src/plugins/loader.ts` | Wire `resolveTrustTier()` at both `createPluginRecord` call sites |
| `src/cli/plugins-cli.ts` | Show trust tier in `plugins info` + verbose list |
| `src/plugins/trust.test.ts` | 17 unit tests — all format × origin combos |
| `docs/plugins/trust-tiers.md` | Design doc with tier definitions, assignment rules, security rationale |
| `docs/plugins/bundles.md` | Cross-reference to trust tiers doc |
| `src/commands/onboarding/plugin-install.test.ts` | Add required `trustTier` to mock record |

## Test plan

- [x] `tsc --noEmit` passes (non-optional `trustTier` caught everywhere)
- [x] 17/17 new unit tests pass (all format × origin × bundleFormat combos)
- [x] Existing test suite unaffected (no regressions)
- [ ] Manual: `openclaw plugins list --verbose` shows trust tier
- [ ] Manual: `openclaw plugins info <bundle-id>` shows "Trust tier: content"
- [ ] Manual: `openclaw plugins info <native-id>` shows "Trust tier: native"

[AI-assisted]

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## 🎯 改进内容

本次 PR 优化了飞书扩展的错误消息处理，提供更好的中文本地化和详细的错误上下文信息。

### 📝 具体改进

#### 1️⃣ 权限错误提示优化 (bot.ts)
- 将技术化英文错误改为友好的中文提示
- 区分有/无授权链接两种情况
- 提供清晰的操作指引

**改进前**:
```
[System: The bot encountered a Feishu API permission error. Please inform the user about this issue and provide the permission grant URL for the admin to authorize. Permission grant URL: xxx]
```

**改进后**:
```
【⚠️ 飞书权限提示】机器人缺少必要的 API 权限，部分功能可能无法使用。
请管理员点击以下链接授权：xxx
（如链接无法点击，请完整复制到浏览器打开。授权后重试即可。）
```

#### 2️⃣ Wiki 操作错误处理增强 (wiki.ts)
为所有 Wiki API 操作添加详细错误上下文，包括操作类型、空间 ID、节点 token 等信息：
- ✅ 获取知识空间列表

- ✅ 移动节点

#### 3️⃣ Bitable 错误消息优化 (bitable.ts)
- 中文化错误消息
- 添加 URL、token 等上下文信息
- 改进用户友好的错误提示

#### 4️⃣ Media 文件处理错误本地化 (media.ts + send-result.ts)
- 图片/文件上传下载错误中文化
- 添加详细的上下文信息（file_key, message_id 等）
- 统一错误前缀格式：`[Media] 操作失败`

#### 5️⃣ 测试用例更新
- 更新所有受影响的测试用例
- 新增测试用例覆盖边界情况
- ✅ **408 个测试全部通过**

### 📊 统计数据

| media.test.ts | +2/-2 | 测试用例更新 |
| **总计** | **+50/-27** | **7 个文件** |

### 🎁 用户收益

- **更好的中文用户体验** - 错误消息清晰易懂
- **更容易调试** - 详细的错误上下文帮助快速定位问题
- **一致的错误格式** - 统一的错误消息格式提升专业度

### ✅ 测试

所有 408 个飞书扩展测试用例全部通过：
```

这是我为成为 OpenClaw 优秀贡献者迈出的第一步！期待 review 和反馈。🦞

## Notes

Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed generated config/plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `4c940bfd8ca68f20f60087b34651a058d9cc95e8`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=4c940bfd8ca68f20f60087b34651a058d9cc95e8 HEAD_SHORT=4c940bfd8c node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-02d093.mjs`

{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}

target.title: Telegram: add reaction notification wake policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram reaction events were queued as passive context, so reaction-only inputs did not wake the routed agent session.
- Why it matters: operators may want selected notification events to wake a routed session, but making every accepted reaction wake by default is a compatibility change.
- What changed: accepted Telegram reaction events now include stable `reaction_key` values for standard and custom emoji and route through shared `notificationWake.reactions` policy.
- Compatibility boundary: reaction events stay queue-only by default; set `channels.telegram.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` to request immediate routed heartbeat wakes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #47677
- Related: #83611
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: accepted Telegram reaction-only events now enqueue stable reaction-key system events and wake the routed session only when the resolved shared notification wake policy is `wake`. Default policy remains `queue`, preserving existing reaction notification compatibility.
- Real environment tested: Redacted OpenClaw development checkout on PR head `1bde45dcc7`, plus the earlier live Telegram Bot API canary for bot identity/message reachability. No secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs are included here.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm tsgo:prod`
  - `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>`
  - `git diff --check`
- Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal verification from PR head:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  159 passed (159)

pnpm config:channels:check
passed

pnpm config:docs:check
OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256

pnpm tsgo:prod
passed

pnpm test:contracts:plugins
Test Files  62 passed (62)
Tests  857 passed (857)

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check
passed
```

Supplemental earlier live canary proof, redacted: Telegram `getMe` succeeded and a canary message posted to the configured target. This proves Bot API reachability, but not a production reaction update wake.
- Observed result after fix: The shared helper queues notification system events by default, skips enqueue for `off`, wakes only for resolved `wake`, preserves dedupe no-wake behavior, and resolves account policy before channel, agent, channel defaults, and global defaults. Telegram handler tests prove queue-by-default, opt-in wake, custom emoji stable keys, trust downgrade, authorization/routing preservation, and no wake when enqueue deduplicates.
- What was not tested: I did not capture a live Telegram Desktop reaction update waking a production routed session after this policy patch. The PR still has the `mantis: telegram-visible-proof` label so maintainer/Mantis proof can cover that external surface.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current main enqueues accepted Telegram reaction system events and returns without requesting a heartbeat wake.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Telegram `message_reaction` handling enqueued system events and then returned without a policy-controlled wake path.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests asserted enqueue behavior but did not assert notification wake policy, default queue compatibility, or deduped no-wake behavior.
- Contributing context (if known): Telegram custom emoji reactions were not represented in the event text because only standard emoji reactions were collected.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Contract test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests/files: `src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
- Scenario the test should lock in: accepted standard/custom emoji reactions enqueue stable reaction-key events, stay queue-only by default, wake only under `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`, and do not wake when enqueue deduplicates.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the shared helper owns policy precedence and heartbeat behavior; the Telegram handler harness owns reaction authorization/routing/enqueue behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Telegram reaction notifications that pass existing access controls enqueue system events by default. Operators can opt into immediate routed wakes with `channels.telegram.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`. System event text includes `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` or `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` so preference/prompt text can assign meaning to specific reactions without adding per-emoji channel schema.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
[Telegram reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [processed on next scheduled/explicit wake]

Opt-in wake:
[Telegram reaction] -> [notificationWake.reactions = wake] -> [system event queued] -> [heartbeat wake] -> [agent sees reaction event]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram handler unit harness and shared notification system-event helper

target.title: Dockerfile: simplify multi-stage build
target.body:
- Reduce from 5 stages to 2 (build + runtime)
- Remove retry loops for Bun and Corepack installs
- Hardcode bookworm-slim runtime (removes variant switching)
- Inline extension copy (removes ext-deps stage)
- Remove SHA256 digest pinning for simpler maintenance
- Preserve all original comments, variable names, and optional features

## Summary

- Problem: The Dockerfile uses 5 build stages, retry loops, variant switching via ARGs, and SHA256 digest pinning, making it difficult to read, maintain, and debug for contributors and self-hosters.
- Why it matters: A simpler Dockerfile lowers the barrier for contributions, speeds up local iteration, and reduces the surface area for build failures caused by stage interdependencies.
- What changed: Consolidated 5 stages into 2 (build + runtime), removed retry loops for Bun/Corepack installs, hardcoded bookworm-slim as the runtime base, replaced the ext-deps stage with a direct COPY, and removed SHA256 digest pinning in favor of standard image tags.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): All variable names (`OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES`, `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BROWSER`, `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_DOCKER_CLI`, `OPENCLAW_DOCKER_GPG_FINGERPRINT`), optional feature blocks (browser, Docker CLI, extra apt packages), security hardening (non-root user, healthcheck), build steps (`pnpm install`, a2ui bundle, build:docker, ui:build, prune), and the runtime CMD are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- The `OPENCLAW_VARIANT` build arg is removed; the runtime image is always based on `node:24-bookworm-slim`. Users who explicitly built with `OPENCLAW_VARIANT=default` (full bookworm) will need to adjust or use the slim image.
- The `OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS` build arg and selective extension filtering are removed; all extensions in the `extensions/` directory are now copied unconditionally.
- Image tags are no longer pinned to SHA256 digests; builds will pull the latest `node:24-bookworm` and `node:24-bookworm-slim` tags.
- Bun and Corepack installs no longer retry on transient failures; builds on flaky networks may need manual retries.
- APT layer caching uses `rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*` instead of named `--mount=type=cache` mounts.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No` - same upstream sources (bun.sh, npm registry, apt repos, Docker GPG key)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (Arch Linux Testing)
- Runtime/container: Docker 29.3.0 with BuildKit/buildx v0.32.1
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): Default build with no optional build args

### Steps

1. `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build -t openclaw-simplified .`
2. `docker run --rm openclaw-simplified node -e "console.log('ok')"`
3. `docker run --rm openclaw-simplified node openclaw.mjs gateway --help`

### Expected

- Image builds successfully in 2 stages
- Container starts and runs Node.js commands
- Gateway help output is displayed

### Actual

- Image built successfully in 2 stages (`build` 15 steps + `stage-1` 16 steps) in ~192s
  - Final image: `openclaw-simplified:latest` - 4.08GB disk / 1.02GB content (sha256:a09af4b5f88c)
- Container starts and runs Node.js: `docker run --rm openclaw-simplified node -e "console.log('ok')"` → `ok`
- Gateway help output displayed: `docker run --rm openclaw-simplified node openclaw.mjs gateway --help` → full help text with all subcommands rendered correctly
- OpenClaw version: `2026.3.14 (unknown)`
- Runtime user: `node` (non-root, uid 1000)

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets [openclaw-build.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26009537/openclaw-build.txt)

Full build + verification log attached: [openclaw-build.log](cci:7://file:///home/szoltysek/openclaw-build.log:0:0-0:0) (228 lines)
Contains: BuildKit build output (39 steps, all succeeded), node smoke test (`ok`), `gateway --help` full output, and `docker images` size info.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Built the image locally with `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build -t openclaw-simplified .`; ran `node -e "console.log('ok')"` in the container; ran `node openclaw.mjs gateway --help` and confirmed full help output; verified image size with `docker images`.
- Edge cases checked: Optional build args (`OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES`, `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BROWSER`, `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_DOCKER_CLI`) are still present and conditionally executed (confirmed by build log showing `if [ -n "" ]` no-op steps); A2UI bundle fallback stub logic is preserved.
- What you did **not** verify: Cross-architecture builds (ARM/QEMU); builds with optional args enabled (`OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BROWSER=1`, `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_DOCKER_CLI=1`); performance comparison against the original 5-stage Dockerfile.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `No` - the `OPENCLAW_VARIANT` and `OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS` build args are removed.
- Config/env changes? `Yes` - users relying on `OPENCLAW_VARIANT=default` (full bookworm) or selective `OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS` filtering need to adjust.
- Migration needed? `Yes`
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  1. If you used `--build-arg OPENCLAW_VARIANT=default`, remove that arg. The runtime is now always bookworm-slim. If you need packages only in full bookworm, add them via `--build-arg OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES="..."`.
  2. If you used `--build-arg OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS="ext1 ext2"`, remove that arg. All extensions are now copied. To exclude extensions, remove them from the `extensions/` directory before building.
  3. If you pinned builds to specific SHA256 digests, note that builds now track the `node:24-bookworm` / `node:24-bookworm-slim` rolling tags.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the single `Dockerfile` to the previous commit: `git revert <commit-sha>`
- Files/config to restore: `Dockerfile` (single file change)
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - `curl: (22) The requested URL returned error: 5xx` during Bun install (no retry loop - re-run the build)
  - Missing extensions at runtime if the `extensions/` directory was not fully populated at build time
  - Larger image size if unneeded extensions are included (previously filtered by OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Bun or Corepack install fails on transient network errors without retry loops.
  - Mitigation: Docker/Buildx layer caching means re-running the build retries only the failed layer. CI systems typically have their own retry mechanisms. The retry loops can be re-added if this proves to be a recurring issue in CI.

- Risk: Removing SHA256 digest pinning means builds are no longer fully reproducible - the `node:24-bookworm` tag can move.
  - Mitigation: For production/release builds, users can pin digests in their own `docker-compose.yml` or CI pipeline. The simplification benefits outweigh the reproducibility trade-off for the majority of users.

- Risk: Removing `OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS` filtering may increase image size if many extensions exist.
  - Mitigation: Extensions are typically small (package.json + JS files). The ext-deps stage only saved space for users with many unused extensions. Users needing minimal images can prune `extensions/` before building.

- Risk: Removing the full bookworm variant may break users who depend on packages only available in the non-slim image.
  - Mitigation: The `OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES` build arg remains available to install any additional packages. The slim image + explicit apt installs is the more common Docker pattern.

target.title: feat(telegram): bi-directional custom emoji reactions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds support for Telegram custom emoji reactions in both directions:

### Inbound — Receive custom emoji reactions
- `bot-handlers.ts`: Now detects `type: "custom_emoji"` reactions alongside standard emoji reactions
- Custom emoji reactions surface as system events with the `custom_emoji_id` in event text
- Context key format: `telegram:reaction:add:{chatId}:{msgId}:{userId}:custom_emoji:{customEmojiId}`
- Mixed updates (both emoji + custom emoji in one reaction update) are handled correctly

### Outbound — Send custom emoji reactions
- `reactMessageTelegram()` accepts optional `customEmojiId` parameter
- When `customEmojiId` is provided, sends `{type: "custom_emoji", custom_emoji_id: "..."}` instead of `{type: "emoji", emoji: "..."}`
- `customEmojiId` takes priority when both emoji and customEmojiId are provided
- `telegram-actions.ts`: The `react` action now accepts `customEmojiId` parameter

### Tests
- 3 new `send.test.ts` cases: custom emoji send, priority over standard emoji, remove with custom emoji
- 3 new `bot.test.ts` cases: custom emoji inbound event, mixed emoji+custom_emoji, removal (no event)

### Notes on Custom Emoji IDs
- Custom emoji reactions require **Telegram Premium** on the reacting user's account
- Custom emoji IDs are numeric strings (e.g., `"5368324170671202286"`)
- To find a custom emoji ID: receive a custom emoji reaction inbound (it will appear in the system event), or use the Telegram Bot API `getCustomEmojiStickers` method
- The bot itself does NOT need Premium to set custom emoji reactions, but the chat must allow them

### Quality
- `npx tsc --noEmit`: No new errors (pre-existing errors in device-pair/chrome-mcp unchanged)
- `npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`: 68/68 pass (3 new)
- `npm run build`: Succeeds

## Summary Comprehensive accessibility overhaul of the web dashboard (Lit Web Components in `ui/`), targeting VoiceOver on Safari. Addresses missing ARIA roles/states, unlabeled form controls, hover-only buttons unreachable by keyboard, lack of heading structure for chat navigation, and a VoiceOver hang on the raw JSON config editor. ### Global CSS infrastructure - Add `.sr-only` utility class (clip-rect technique) for screen-reader-only content - Add `.skip-link` styles (sr-only by default, visible on `:focus`) for keyboard skip navigation - Replace theme-only `prefers-reduced-motion` rule with a universal one that suppresses all animations and transitions ### Raw JSON config editor VoiceOver fix - Cap `.config-raw-field textarea` at `max-height: 80vh; overflow-y: auto` to prevent unbounded textarea growth that causes VoiceOver to hang - Add `aria-label="Raw JSON5 configuration editor"` and `aria-roledescription="code editor"` on the raw textarea ### Config form field accessibility - Add `fieldId()` helper for generating stable `id`/`for` associations - Boolean toggles: `role="switch"`, `aria-checked`, `aria-label` - Text/number/select/textarea inputs: `id`/`for` label-input linking, `aria-describedby` for help text - Segmented enum controls: `role="radiogroup"` on container, `role="radio"` and `aria-checked` on each button - `aria-label` on reset, decrease, increase, remove item, and remove entry buttons - `aria-label="Search settings"` and `aria-label="Clear search"` on config search ### Chat message headings and button accessibility - Add sr-only headings for VoiceOver rotor heading navigation with semantic hierarchy: - `

target.title: fix(gateway/discord): respect env proxy vars and prevent ECONNRESET

## Summary

- Bootstrap global `EnvHttpProxyAgent` in gateway startup so Node's `fetch()` honors `https_proxy`/`HTTP_PROXY` env vars — fixes Discord REST calls (command deployment, bot identity fetch) timing out behind local proxies
- Discord gateway plugin now falls back to env proxy vars for WebSocket connections when no explicit `channels.discord.proxy` is configured

target.title: fix(logs): default to local timezone for log timestamps
target.body:
## Summary

Log timestamps now display in the local timezone by default. Added `--utc` to opt into UTC display while keeping the previous `--local-time` flag accepted as a compatibility no-op.

Closes #46748

## Problem

`openclaw logs` displayed timestamps in UTC when the user's system was in a non-UTC timezone. Existing callers could also still pass `--local-time`, so the compatibility flag should continue to be accepted after making local time the default.

## What changed

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| Default timestamp display used UTC | Default timestamp display uses local timezone |
| `--local-time` selected local display | `--local-time` remains accepted as a no-op compatibility flag |
| No explicit UTC opt-in in this path | `--utc` opts into UTC display |

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
`openclaw logs --local-time` should be accepted by the real CLI parser and should proceed into the logs command instead of failing with an unknown-option error.

Real environment tested:
Local macOS checkout of OpenClaw on branch `pr-47365-ci-fix`, Node 24.10.0, pnpm 11.1.0.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
`PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm openclaw logs --local-time --limit 1 --plain --url ws://127.0.0.1:9 --timeout 250`

Evidence after fix:
```text
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs logs --local-time --limit 1 --plain --url ws://127.0.0.1:9 --timeout 250
Gateway not reachable. Is it running and accessible?
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:9
Source: cli --url
Config: /Users/efebarandurmaz/.openclaw/openclaw.json
Hint: run `openclaw doctor`.
[ELIFECYCLE] Command failed with exit code 1.
```

Observed result after fix:
The command reaches gateway connection handling with the explicit `--local-time` flag present. It no longer exits at argument parsing with `error: unknown option '--local-time'`.

What was not tested:
A running gateway log stream was not exercised locally; this pass verifies the real CLI compatibility path and timestamp-mode parser behavior.

## Test plan

- [x] `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm tsgo:core`
- [x] `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- [x] `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm exec vitest run src/cli/logs-cli.test.ts`
- [x] `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm openclaw logs --local-time --limit 1 --plain --url ws://127.0.0.1:9 --timeout 250`

target.title: feat: add Azure Claude (AI Foundry) onboarding path
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Users deploying Claude via Azure AI Foundry have no onboarding path — they must manually configure provider settings, auth profiles, and model refs.
- **Why it matters:** Azure Claude is a common enterprise deployment target; first-class onboarding removes friction for these users.
- **What changed:** Added `anthropic-azure-api-key` as a new auth choice with interactive and non-interactive flows (resource/URL prompt, model picker, API key storage, config wiring).
- **What did NOT change:** No changes to existing Anthropic direct API flows, no new runtime dependencies, no changes to gateway or startup critical path.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Docs

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Auth / tokens
- [x] UI / DX

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New auth choice `anthropic-azure-api-key` appears in the onboarding provider picker under the Anthropic group
- New CLI flags: `--anthropic-azure-api-key`, `--anthropic-azure-base-url`, `--anthropic-azure-model-id`
- New env vars supported: `ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY`, `AZURE_CLAUDE_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_BASE_URL`, `AZURE_CLAUDE_BASE_URL`, `ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_RESOURCE`, `AZURE_CLAUDE_RESOURCE`
- Invalid resource names (e.g. `fabric_hub` with underscores) are rejected with a clear error instead of silently stripped

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `Yes` — new `setAnthropicAzureApiKey` stores Azure API keys using the existing auth profile credential infrastructure (same pattern as all other providers)
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- Mitigation: Uses the same `SecretInput` and `AuthProfileStore` infrastructure as existing providers; no new storage mechanisms.

## Repro + Verification

### Steps

```bash
# Interactive
openclaw onboard --auth-choice anthropic-azure-api-key

# Non-interactive
openclaw onboard --non-interactive --accept-risk \
  --auth-choice anthropic-azure-api-key \
  --anthropic-azure-base-url fabric-hub \
  --anthropic-azure-model-id claude-sonnet-4-6 \
  --anthropic-azure-api-key "$ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY"
```

target.title: fix(security): extend skill scanner to cover Python and shell scripts
target.body:
## Problem

The skill scanner (`src/security/skill-scanner.ts`) only scans JavaScript and TypeScript files, but skills frequently include Python (`.py`) and shell (`.sh`, `.bash`) scripts. These go completely unscanned, creating a blind spot for malicious skills.

For example, the built-in skills directory already contains Python scripts in `model-usage`, `openai-image-gen`, `skill-creator`, and others.

## Changes

**Extended scannable file types** to include `.py`, `.sh`, and `.bash`.

**Added Python-specific detection rules:**
- `dangerous-exec-python` (critical): `subprocess.call/run/Popen` with shell=True, `os.system/popen/exec*`
- `dynamic-code-execution-python` (critical): `exec()`, `eval()`, `compile()` with Python file context guard to reduce false positives
- `dangerous-import-python` (warn): `import ctypes`, `import pickle`, `__import__()`
- `network-python` (warn): `urllib.request`, `requests.post/get`, `httpx`, `socket.connect`
- `env-harvesting-python` (critical): `os.environ` + network send patterns
- `potential-exfiltration-python` (warn): file `open()` + network send patterns
- `obfuscated-code-python` (warn): base64 decode + exec/eval

**Added shell-specific detection rules:**
- `dangerous-eval-shell` (critical): `eval` command usage
- `suspicious-curl-shell` (warn): `curl | sh` and `curl -o` to sensitive system paths
- `suspicious-download-shell` (warn): `wget` targeting IP addresses or URL shorteners
- `env-exfiltration-shell` (critical): `env`/`printenv` piped to `curl`/`wget`/`nc`

**Added 230 lines of tests** covering every new rule with positive detection and negative (no false positive) cases.

## Design decisions

- Used `requiresContext` guards where appropriate to minimize false positives (e.g., Python `exec()` only flags when the file looks like actual Python, not a JS file mentioning exec)
- Kept all existing JS/TS rules untouched
- Followed the existing LineRule/SourceRule pattern exactly
- Shell rules are conservative to avoid flagging legitimate admin scripts

target.title: fix: respect totalTokensFresh flag to avoid showing stale token counts
target.body:
## Problem
When a session's `totalTokensFresh` flag is `false`, the `totalTokens` value may be stale/historical (e.g., accumulated from previous compaction) and should not be displayed.

This caused incorrect displays like "100% context used 2.6M / 200k" in both TUI and Web UI when the token count was stale.

## Root Cause
The Gateway already correctly filters stale token counts via `resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens`, but:
1. **TUI**: Type definition was missing `totalTokensFresh` field, so TUI couldn't check it
2. **UI**: `formatSessionTokens` function didn't check the freshness flag

## Solution

### TUI (commit 368ca03f1)
- Add `totalTokensFresh` to `GatewaySessionList` type in `gateway-chat.ts`
- Add `totalTokensFresh` to `SessionInfoEntry` type in `tui-session-actions.ts`
- Check `totalTokensFresh !== false` before updating `sessionInfo.totalTokens`

### UI (commit 446fdd13a)
- Add `totalTokensFresh` to `GatewaySessionRow` type in `types.ts`
- Check `totalTokensFresh === false` in `formatSessionTokens` function, return "n/a" when stale

## Testing
- TypeScript compilation passes for both TUI and UI
- The fix ensures stale token counts are not displayed in sessions list

## Files Changed
- `src/tui/gateway-chat.ts` - Add `totalTokensFresh` to type definition
- `src/tui/tui-session-actions.ts` - Add type and check before using `totalTokens`
- `ui/src/ui/types.ts` - Add `totalTokensFresh` to type definition
- `ui/src/ui/presenter.ts` - Check freshness before displaying tokens

target.title: feat(feishu): enable real-time character-by-character streaming for subagent runs
target.body:
## AI Disclosure
Built with Antigravity (powered by Gemini) and refined with Claude.  
Ran AI review passes as a sanity check before submitting.  

Fully tested — verified real-time streaming for both main agent and subagent lanes.

---

## Summary

### Problem
Previously, character-by-character streaming was only optimized for the main agent run. When a subagent was spawned, its output would often arrive as a single bulk message or be delayed, leading to a “frozen” user experience during complex background tasks.

### Why it matters
Subagents are core to complex background task execution. A lack of real-time feedback makes it hard for users to track progress, causing UX frustration. Good observability is essential for trust in agentic workflows.

### What changed (Core Feat: Subagent Streaming Bridge)

- **Architectural Hook**  
  Introduced `createOutboundDispatcher` to the `ChannelPlugin` interface.  
  This allows subagent runs to tap into the same real-time UI streaming infrastructure as the main agent.

- **Feishu Implementation**  
  Bridged subagent output directly to `FeishuStreamingSession`.  
  Subagents now stream text character-by-character with parity to the main agent.

- **Advanced UI Polish**
  - **Tool Animations**: Dynamic status lines showing which tool is active (e.g., `💻 exec`, `🤖 sessions_spawn`, `🌍 web_search`)
  - **Reasoning Process**: Real-time display of the "Thinking" block within the Feishu card
  - **UX Enhancements**: Integrated rich iconography (emojis) for all standard tool mappings

---

## Technical Details

- **Sync with Upstream**  
  Fully rebased onto the latest `upstream/main`, adapting to the new asynchronous `markDispatchIdle` and `replaceContent` interfaces.

- **Atomic Rendering**  
  Switched to `replaceContent` in Feishu to ensure clean transitions between tool status, reasoning blocks, and final answers (preventing UI ghosting).

- **Clean History**  
  Consolidated into a single, clean feature commit to maintain repository health.

---

## Verification Plan

### Automated Tests
Verified TypeScript build/lint success:

```bash
npm run lint
npm run type-check
```

### Manual Verification

- **Subagent Streaming**  
  Spawned subagents in a Feishu group chat and verified that the output streams character-by-character.

- **Tool UI**  
  Confirmed that the "Thinking" and tool-specific emojis display correctly during execution.

- **Concurrency**  
  Verified that multiple streaming cards (main + subagent) can be managed simultaneously without state collision.

target.title: fix(exec-approvals): preserve outbound tool context for forwarded not…
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve outbound tool context for forwarded exec-approval notices
- pass target-derived currentChannelId/currentChannelProvider through outbound delivery
- add regression coverage for forwarded request/resolved deliveries

## Problem
When multiple channels are enabled (for example telegram + line), resolving an exec approval could fail during forwarded follow-up delivery with:

`Channel is required when multiple channels are configured: telegram, line`

The exec-approval forwarder already knew the resolved delivery target, but it forwarded notices through outbound delivery without preserving channel threading/tool context. Downstream routing then lost the current channel identity in multi-channel setups.

## Fix
- extend outbound delivery context to carry optional `toolContext`
- thread that context into channel outbound adapter calls
- have exec approval forwarding derive `currentChannelId` and `currentChannelProvider` from the resolved target when sending forwarded notices

## Validation
- `pnpm test -- --run src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
